policy.label: Intellectual Property Rights
policy.description: Intellectual Property Rights (IPR) pertain to legal protections granted to individuals or organizations over the creations of their minds. These rights enable the holder to control the use of their inventions, designs, and artistic works, including copyrights, patents, trademarks, and trade secrets.
policy.permissions: {"action":"reuse","constraint":{"name":"attribution"},"description":"Reuse is permitted provided that proper credit is given to the original creator. This includes indicating the source and providing relevant citations."}; {"action":"share","constraint":{"name":"purpose","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"nonCommercial"},"description":"The intellectual property can be shared with others for non-commercial purposes only, meaning it cannot be sold or used for profit-generating activities."}; {"action":"modify","constraint":{"name":"consent"},"description":"Modifications or adaptations of the intellectual property are allowed only if prior consent is obtained from the rights holder."}
policy.prohibitions: {"action":"commercialUse","constraint":{"name":"consent","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"The intellectual property cannot be used for commercial purposes without explicit permission from the rights holder. This includes selling or licensing the content."}; {"action":"redistribute","constraint":{"name":"attribution","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Redistribution of the intellectual property is prohibited unless appropriate attribution is provided to the original creator."}
policy.duties: {"action":"provideAttribution","description":"Users must provide clear and accurate attribution when using, sharing, or modifying the intellectual property. Attribution includes identifying the original creator and the source of the work."}; {"action":"respectMoralRights","description":"Users must respect the moral rights of the creator, ensuring that the work is not altered in a way that could harm the creator’s reputation or integrity."}
policy.sources: {"name":"World Intellectual Property Organization (WIPO)","description":"Provides comprehensive details about copyrights, patents, trademarks, and other intellectual property rights.","link":"https://www.wipo.int"}; {"name":"Berne Convention for the Protection of Literary and Artistic Works","description":"Provides protection for authors' rights on literary and artistic works.","link":"https://www.wipo.int/treaties/en/ip/berne/"}; {"name":"European Union Intellectual Property Office (EUIPO)","description":"Offers information on intellectual property rights within the EU.","link":"https://euipo.europa.eu"}

policy.description: Personal Data Rights refer to the rights individuals have regarding the protection and privacy of their personal data. These rights are governed by regulations such as the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR), ensuring that personal data is collected, processed, and stored lawfully, and individuals maintain control over how their personal data is used.
policy.permissions: {"action":"access","constraint":{"name":"consent","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"true"},"description":"Access to personal data is allowed only if explicit consent is provided by the data subject. This includes the right of individuals to access their data and understand how it is being used."}; {"action":"store","constraint":{"name":"dataMinimization"},"description":"Personal data can only be stored if it is relevant and limited to the necessary purposes for which it was collected, following the principle of data minimization."}
policy.prohibitions: {"action":"share","constraint":{"name":"consent","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Sharing or distributing personal data is prohibited unless explicit consent has been given by the data subject as required by data protection regulations."}; {"action":"process","constraint":{"name":"legalBasis","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Processing of personal data without a legal basis, such as consent or contractual necessity, is prohibited."}
policy.duties: {"action":"requestConsent","description":"Data controllers must obtain clear, informed, and unambiguous consent from the data subject before collecting or processing personal data."}; {"action":"ensureDataSecurity","description":"Data controllers are obligated to implement appropriate technical and organizational measures to ensure the security of personal data, protecting it from unauthorized access, breaches, or misuse."}
policy.sources: {"name":"General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR)","description":"The primary regulation governing personal data protection in the European Union.","link":"https://gdpr-info.eu/"}; {"name":"European Data Protection Board (EDPB)","description":"Provides guidelines and information on data protection laws and GDPR compliance in the EU.","link":"https://edpb.europa.eu/"}; {"name":"EOSC Privacy Guidelines","description":"EOSC-specific guidelines on personal data protection and privacy in the context of the open science cloud.","link":"https://eosc-portal.eu/"}

policy.description: Ethical Rights refer to the moral rights that protect the integrity of a creator's work and ensure that the creator receives proper recognition. These rights include the right to attribution and the right to object to any derogatory treatment or distortion of the work. Ethical rights are often connected to the preservation of the creator's personal and professional reputation.
policy.permissions: {"action":"use","constraint":{"name":"respectIntegrity"},"description":"The use of the work is allowed as long as it respects the creator's intentions and does not damage their reputation or integrity. This includes appropriate attribution and acknowledgment of the creator."}; {"action":"quote","constraint":{"name":"purpose","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"fairUse"},"description":"Short quotations from the work are permitted for fair use purposes, such as research, education, or commentary, provided that proper attribution is given."}
policy.prohibitions: {"action":"distort","constraint":{"name":"consent","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Any alteration or distortion of the work that misrepresents the creator's original intent is prohibited unless explicit consent is given."}; {"action":"failToCredit","constraint":{"name":"attribution","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Failing to credit the creator when using their work, particularly in research or publication contexts, is prohibited."}
policy.duties: {"action":"provideAttribution","description":"Users of the work must provide clear attribution to the creator, acknowledging their contribution and ensuring the work is credited properly."}; {"action":"respectIntegrity","description":"Users must respect the moral integrity of the creator’s work and refrain from actions that could distort or misrepresent the original content or intent."}
policy.sources: {"name":"Berne Convention for the Protection of Literary and Artistic Works","description":"Protects the moral rights of creators, ensuring attribution and integrity of their work.","link":"https://www.wipo.int/treaties/en/ip/berne/"}; {"name":"Creative Commons - Moral Rights","description":"Explains the concept of moral rights in the context of Creative Commons licenses, focusing on attribution and integrity.","link":"https://creativecommons.org/"}; {"name":"EOSC Code of Conduct","description":"EOSC-specific guidelines that ensure ethical handling of research outputs and proper attribution of creators in open science contexts.","link":"https://eosc-portal.eu/"}

policy.description: Data Usage Rights refer to the permissions and restrictions regarding the use, sharing, and distribution of data. These rights ensure that the data is handled in accordance with legal, ethical, and licensing requirements, with a focus on ensuring transparency and accountability in its usage.
policy.permissions: {"action":"use","constraint":{"name":"license"},"description":"The use of data is allowed provided that it complies with the terms and conditions of the applicable license. Proper attribution to the data owner must be given."}; {"action":"share","constraint":{"name":"purpose","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"nonCommercial"},"description":"Sharing of data is permitted for non-commercial purposes only, as long as it is not altered, and proper credit is provided to the original owner."}
policy.prohibitions: {"action":"commercialUse","constraint":{"name":"license","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Commercial use of data is prohibited unless explicitly allowed by the license provided by the data owner."}; {"action":"alter","constraint":{"name":"consent","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Altering the data is prohibited unless consent is explicitly given by the data owner."}
policy.duties: {"action":"provideAttribution","description":"Users must provide proper attribution to the data owner when using, sharing, or distributing the data."}; {"action":"adhereToLicense","description":"Users must ensure that they follow the terms of the license under which the data is provided."}
policy.sources: {"name":"Creative Commons – Data Usage Rights","description":"Provides guidelines and examples on how to apply Creative Commons licenses to data and ensure proper attribution.","link":"https://creativecommons.org/"}; {"name":"EOSC Guidelines on Data Sharing","description":"Guidelines provided by the EOSC for data sharing and usage in open science contexts.","link":"https://eosc-portal.eu/"}; {"name":"OpenAIRE Licensing Guide","description":"A guide from OpenAIRE on how to license and share research data under various licensing schemes.","link":"https://www.openaire.eu/"}

policy.description: Scientific Research Rights govern the use, sharing, and publication of scientific research outputs. These rights aim to ensure ethical practices, promote transparency, and encourage open access to research funded by public resources.
policy.permissions: {"action":"disseminate","constraint":{"name":"repository","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"openAccess"},"description":"Researchers are allowed to disseminate their work if it is shared through open-access repositories, ensuring accessibility for the wider community."}; {"action":"reuse","constraint":{"name":"purpose","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"educationalResearch"},"description":"Reuse of research outputs is permitted for educational and research purposes as long as proper attribution is provided."}
policy.prohibitions: {"action":"plagiarize","constraint":{"name":"originality","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Plagiarizing any part of the research, including text, data, or methodology, is strictly prohibited."}; {"action":"restrictAccess","constraint":{"name":"fundingSource","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"public"},"description":"Restricting access to publicly funded research outputs is not allowed. Researchers are obligated to ensure public accessibility."}
policy.duties: {"action":"provideAttribution","description":"Researchers must provide proper attribution to the original authors and sources when using prior work in their research."}; {"action":"publishOpenAccess","description":"Researchers are required to publish their outputs in open-access repositories when mandated by their funding bodies or institutions."}
policy.sources: {"name":"OpenAIRE – Open Science Framework","description":"Guidelines for managing and sharing scientific research in open science ecosystems.","link":"https://www.openaire.eu/"}; {"name":"EOSC Charter of Open Science Principles","description":"Outlines the principles and best practices for open science in EOSC.","link":"https://eosc-portal.eu/"}; {"name":"COPE (Committee on Publication Ethics)","description":"Provides ethical guidelines for research publication, focusing on integrity and transparency.","link":"https://publicationethics.org/"}

policy.description: Licensing Framework Rights define the permissions, prohibitions, and obligations associated with applying and using licenses for digital and research content. These frameworks ensure that users respect the terms of use, distribution, and adaptation while promoting compliance and transparency.
policy.permissions: {"action":"distribute","constraint":{"name":"licenseCompliance"},"description":"Distribution of the content is permitted as long as the user complies with the terms and conditions of the applicable license."}; {"action":"adapt","constraint":{"name":"licenseType","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"CreativeCommonsBY-SA"},"description":"Adaptation of the content is allowed under specific licenses, such as Creative Commons BY-SA, provided that attribution is given and the adaptations are shared under similar terms."}
policy.prohibitions: {"action":"misuseLicense","constraint":{"name":"licenseCompliance","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Misusing or violating the terms of the license, such as omitting attribution or exceeding the scope of permissions, is prohibited."}; {"action":"revokeLicense","constraint":{"name":"legalJustification","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Revoking a license without proper legal justification or agreement from the licensee is prohibited."}
policy.duties: {"action":"displayLicense","description":"Users must ensure that license information is prominently displayed along with the content, enabling others to understand the terms of use."}; {"action":"ensureCompliance","description":"Users are obligated to align their actions with the terms and conditions specified in the applied license."}
policy.sources: {"name":"Creative Commons Licensing Guide","description":"A comprehensive guide on using and understanding Creative Commons licenses for digital and research outputs.","link":"https://creativecommons.org/"}; {"name":"Open Definition by Open Knowledge Foundation","description":"Explains the principles and standards for open licenses, including data and content sharing.","link":"https://opendefinition.org/"}; {"name":"EOSC Licensing Guidelines","description":"EOSC-specific recommendations for applying licenses to research outputs within the open science ecosystem.","link":"https://eosc-portal.eu/"}

policy.description: Access and Reuse Rights refer to the permissions and obligations associated with accessing and reusing digital content and research outputs. These rights are designed to encourage openness, ensure compliance with terms of use, and promote responsible and ethical reuse.
policy.permissions: {"action":"access","constraint":{"name":"availability","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"public"},"description":"Access to content is permitted if it is publicly available or if explicit permission has been granted by the owner or license holder."}; {"action":"reuse","constraint":{"name":"licenseCompliance"},"description":"Reuse of content is permitted under the terms of the applicable license, provided that proper attribution is given to the original source."}
policy.prohibitions: {"action":"unauthorizedAccess","constraint":{"name":"permission","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Accessing restricted or confidential content without explicit authorization from the content owner is prohibited."}; {"action":"improperReuse","constraint":{"name":"licenseCompliance","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Reusing content in ways that violate license terms or ethical guidelines is prohibited."}
policy.duties: {"action":"acknowledgeSource","description":"Users must provide proper attribution to the original source when accessing or reusing content, ensuring credit is given to the creators or owners."}; {"action":"followLicenseTerms","description":"Users must comply with the license terms under which the content is made available, ensuring responsible and lawful use."}
policy.sources: {"name":"Creative Commons – Access and Reuse Rights","description":"Explains how Creative Commons licenses facilitate access and reuse while ensuring proper attribution and compliance.","link":"https://creativecommons.org/"}; {"name":"OpenAIRE Access Guidelines","description":"OpenAIRE’s guidelines for ensuring open access and responsible reuse of research outputs.","link":"https://www.openaire.eu/"}; {"name":"EOSC FAIR Principles","description":"EOSC’s FAIR (Findable, Accessible, Interoperable, Reusable) principles, emphasizing the responsible sharing and reuse of digital assets.","link":"https://eosc-portal.eu/"}

policy.description: Ethical Research and Integrity Rights emphasize the importance of transparency, accountability, and adherence to ethical guidelines in the creation, use, and dissemination of scientific research. These rights safeguard the integrity of research processes and outputs.
policy.permissions: {"action":"conductResearch","constraint":{"name":"ethicsApproval","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"true"},"description":"Researchers are permitted to conduct research provided that ethical guidelines are followed, and the research has been approved by relevant authorities or ethics boards."}; {"action":"collaborate","constraint":{"name":"mutualAgreement"},"description":"Collaboration among researchers is permitted as long as mutual agreements are in place, and all contributors receive proper attribution."}
policy.prohibitions: {"action":"fabricateData","constraint":{"name":"dataAuthenticity","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Fabricating or falsifying data is strictly prohibited as it undermines the integrity of research outputs."}; {"action":"misrepresentResearch","constraint":{"name":"honesty","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Misrepresenting research findings or methods in a way that misleads stakeholders is prohibited."}
policy.duties: {"action":"ensureAccuracy","description":"Researchers are obligated to ensure that their data and results are accurate, reliable, and reproducible to maintain the credibility of their research."}; {"action":"followEthicalStandards","description":"Researchers must adhere to established ethical standards and institutional policies governing their work."}
policy.sources: {"name":"COPE (Committee on Publication Ethics)","description":"Guidelines for ethical research and publication practices, focusing on transparency and accountability.","link":"https://publicationethics.org/"}; {"name":"EOSC Ethics and Integrity Framework","description":"Outlines ethical principles for conducting and sharing research in the EOSC ecosystem.","link":"https://eosc-portal.eu/"}; {"name":"Helsinki Declaration (WMA)","description":"Ethical principles for medical research involving human subjects, widely applied across disciplines.","link":"https://www.wma.net/policies-post/wma-declaration-of-helsinki-ethical-principles-for-medical-research-involving-human-subjects/"}

policy.description: Data Privacy and Protection Rights safeguard the collection, processing, storage, and sharing of personal data, ensuring compliance with regulations such as GDPR. These rights promote transparency, accountability, and respect for individuals' privacy.
policy.permissions: {"action":"collectData","constraint":{"name":"consent","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"true"},"description":"The collection of personal data is permitted only if explicit consent is provided by the data subject."}; {"action":"storeData","constraint":{"name":"dataMinimization","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"true"},"description":"Storing personal data is allowed if it complies with the principles of data minimization, ensuring that only necessary data is retained."}
policy.prohibitions: {"action":"shareWithoutConsent","constraint":{"name":"consent","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Sharing personal data without the explicit consent of the data subject is strictly prohibited."}; {"action":"processWithoutLegalBasis","constraint":{"name":"legalBasis","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Processing personal data without a legitimate purpose or legal basis is prohibited."}
policy.duties: {"action":"informDataSubjects","description":"Controllers must inform individuals about how their data is collected, processed, and stored, including the purpose of processing and their rights."}; {"action":"ensureDataSecurity","description":"Controllers are required to implement appropriate security measures to protect personal data from breaches or unauthorized access."}
policy.sources: {"name":"General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR)","description":"The primary regulation governing personal data protection in the EU, ensuring transparency and individual rights.","link":"https://gdpr-info.eu/"}; {"name":"European Data Protection Board (EDPB)","description":"Provides guidance on implementing GDPR and ensuring compliance across member states.","link":"https://edpb.europa.eu/"}; {"name":"EOSC Privacy Guidelines","description":"EOSC-specific guidance on ensuring privacy and data protection in open science environments.","link":"https://eosc-portal.eu/"}

policy.description: Open Access Rights outline the permissions, prohibitions, and obligations associated with providing and using open access to research outputs, data, and publications. These rights aim to ensure transparency, accessibility, and adherence to open science principles.
policy.permissions: {"action":"access","constraint":{"name":"availability","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"public"},"description":"Publicly funded research outputs and data are accessible to all, ensuring transparency and widespread dissemination of knowledge."}; {"action":"reuse","constraint":{"name":"licenseCompliance","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"true"},"description":"Reuse of open access content is allowed under the terms of the applicable open license, provided proper attribution is given to the original authors."}
policy.prohibitions: {"action":"restrictAccess","constraint":{"name":"fundingSource","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"public"},"description":"Restricting access to publicly funded research outputs is prohibited unless specific exceptions apply, such as confidentiality or embargo periods."}; {"action":"omitAttribution","constraint":{"name":"attribution","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Reusing open access content without providing proper attribution to the original authors is strictly prohibited."}
policy.duties: {"action":"depositInRepositories","description":"Researchers are obligated to deposit their research outputs in recognized open access repositories to ensure accessibility and compliance with funding requirements."}; {"action":"provideAttribution","description":"Users must acknowledge the original authors and sources when using or distributing open access content."}
policy.sources: {"name":"Open Access Policy by UNESCO","description":"Guidelines for promoting open access to knowledge and research outputs globally.","link":"https://en.unesco.org/open-access"}; {"name":"Plan S Guidelines","description":"Principles for ensuring open access to publicly funded research outputs in the EU.","link":"https://www.coalition-s.org/"}; {"name":"EOSC Open Access Framework","description":"EOSC-specific guidance for implementing open access principles within the open science ecosystem.","link":"https://eosc-portal.eu/"}

policy.description: Licensing and Rights Clearance governs the application, management, and communication of licenses for digital content and research outputs. These rights ensure compliance with licensing terms and facilitate proper permissions for use.
policy.permissions: {"action":"applyLicenses","constraint":{"name":"contentOwnership","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"true"},"description":"Content owners are allowed to apply licenses that define the terms for usage, distribution, and modification of their work."}; {"action":"grantPermissions","constraint":{"name":"rightsHolderApproval"},"description":"Rights holders can provide explicit permissions for uses not covered under the applied license terms."}
policy.prohibitions: {"action":"ignoreLicenseTerms","constraint":{"name":"licenseCompliance","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Ignoring or violating the terms of a license, such as failing to provide attribution, is strictly prohibited."}; {"action":"misrepresentRights","constraint":{"name":"ownership","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Misrepresenting ownership or falsely claiming rights clearance for content is prohibited."}
policy.duties: {"action":"ensureRightsClearance","description":"Users must verify that they have obtained the necessary permissions or licenses to use content before proceeding with any actions."}; {"action":"communicateLicenseTerms","description":"Rights holders must clearly state and communicate the terms of any applied licenses, ensuring transparency and compliance."}
policy.sources: {"name":"Creative Commons Licensing Guide","description":"A comprehensive guide to applying and understanding licenses for digital and research outputs.","link":"https://creativecommons.org/"}; {"name":"WIPO Licensing Framework","description":"Guidelines for managing intellectual property licenses and ensuring compliance with international standards.","link":"https://www.wipo.int/"}; {"name":"EOSC Rights Clearance Guidelines","description":"EOSC-specific guidance for managing rights clearance and licensing in open science contexts.","link":"https://eosc-portal.eu/"}

policy.description: Intellectual Property Management governs the processes and responsibilities associated with establishing, protecting, and managing intellectual property rights for digital content and research outputs. This ensures compliance with IP laws, proper attribution, and transparency in IP usage.
policy.permissions: {"action":"registerIP","constraint":{"name":"contentCreation","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"true"},"description":"Content creators are allowed to register their intellectual property to establish ownership and protect their rights under applicable laws."}; {"action":"transferRights","constraint":{"name":"mutualAgreement"},"description":"Rights holders can transfer ownership of their intellectual property to another party under mutual agreement and proper documentation."}
policy.prohibitions: {"action":"infringeIPRights","constraint":{"name":"authorization","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Using, reproducing, or distributing intellectual property without authorization from the rights holder is strictly prohibited."}; {"action":"claimFalseOwnership","constraint":{"name":"ownershipProof","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Falsely claiming ownership of intellectual property is prohibited, as it undermines legal and ethical principles."}
policy.duties: {"action":"documentOwnership","description":"Rights holders must maintain accurate and verifiable documentation to establish their ownership of intellectual property."}; {"action":"monitorCompliance","description":"Rights holders are responsible for monitoring the use of their intellectual property to identify and address potential infringements."}
policy.sources: {"name":"World Intellectual Property Organization (WIPO)","description":"Provides comprehensive resources and guidelines for managing IP globally.","link":"https://www.wipo.int/"}; {"name":"Berne Convention","description":"International agreement protecting literary and artistic works, including guidelines for IP management.","link":"https://www.wipo.int/treaties/en/ip/berne/"}; {"name":"EOSC IP Management Guidelines","description":"EOSC-specific recommendations for managing IP within the open science ecosystem.","link":"https://eosc-portal.eu/"}

policy.description: Collaborative Research Rights define the permissions, prohibitions, and obligations involved in joint research projects. These rights ensure transparency, equitable attribution, and adherence to ethical and legal standards.
policy.permissions: {"action":"shareResources","constraint":{"name":"mutualAgreement","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"true"},"description":"Collaborators are permitted to share data, tools, and methods under mutually agreed terms, fostering cooperation and transparency."}; {"action":"coPublish","constraint":{"name":"attribution","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"true"},"description":"Co-publishing research outputs is allowed, provided that all contributors are acknowledged, and there is consensus among collaborators."}
policy.prohibitions: {"action":"excludeContributors","constraint":{"name":"justification","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Excluding collaborators from acknowledgment without proper justification or agreement is prohibited."}; {"action":"misappropriateData","constraint":{"name":"usageAgreement","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Using shared data or tools outside the agreed terms of collaboration is prohibited."}
policy.duties: {"action":"acknowledgeContributions","description":"All team members must acknowledge the contributions of collaborators, ensuring equitable recognition of individual efforts."}; {"action":"defineRoles","description":"Teams are obligated to clearly define roles and responsibilities to ensure fair participation and accountability among collaborators."}
policy.sources: {"name":"OECD Principles for Access to Research Data","description":"Guidelines for responsible data sharing and collaboration in research.","link":"https://www.oecd.org/"}; {"name":"EOSC Collaborative Research Guidelines","description":"EOSC-specific guidance for managing collaborations in open science environments.","link":"https://eosc-portal.eu/"}; {"name":"Vancouver Guidelines (ICMJE)","description":"Ethical guidelines for authorship and collaboration in scientific research.","link":"https://www.icmje.org/"}

policy.description: Data Interoperability and Sharing Rights govern the permissions, prohibitions, and obligations necessary to ensure seamless data sharing and integration across platforms, disciplines, and stakeholders. These rights are guided by FAIR principles and compliance with legal and ethical standards.
policy.permissions: {"action":"shareData","constraint":{"name":"interoperability","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"true"},"description":"Sharing data is permitted when formats and metadata comply with recognized standards that enable interoperability."}; {"action":"integrateData","constraint":{"name":"platformCompatibility"},"description":"Integration of datasets across compatible platforms is allowed under agreed terms and conditions, fostering collaboration and reuse."}
policy.prohibitions: {"action":"useIncompatibleFormats","constraint":{"name":"interoperability","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Sharing or storing data in formats that hinder interoperability and reuse is prohibited."}; {"action":"ignoreMetadataStandards","constraint":{"name":"metadataCompliance","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Omitting required metadata that ensures accurate data interpretation and integration is prohibited."}
policy.duties: {"action":"provideMetadata","description":"Data providers must include detailed and accurate metadata to ensure interoperability, reuse, and proper attribution."}; {"action":"adhereToStandards","description":"Users and data providers must comply with recognized standards for data formats and metadata to enable seamless sharing and integration."}
policy.sources: {"name":"FAIR Principles (GO FAIR Initiative)","description":"Guidelines for ensuring data is Findable, Accessible, Interoperable, and Reusable.","link":"https://www.go-fair.org/"}; {"name":"EOSC Interoperability Guidelines","description":"EOSC-specific recommendations for achieving data interoperability and sharing in open science environments.","link":"https://eosc-portal.eu/"}; {"name":"OpenAIRE Data Sharing Standards","description":"OpenAIRE’s guidelines for enabling data sharing and interoperability within the research community.","link":"https://www.openaire.eu/"}

policy.description: Ethical Data Sharing Rights outline the permissions, prohibitions, and obligations involved in sharing data responsibly. These rights emphasize privacy, consent, and respect for individuals and communities, promoting ethical practices in data sharing.
policy.permissions: {"action":"shareAnonymizedData","constraint":{"name":"dataAnonymization","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"true"},"description":"Sharing data is allowed when it is anonymized, ensuring that individuals cannot be identified from the data."}; {"action":"useDataForEthicalPurposes","constraint":{"name":"ethicalCompliance","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"true"},"description":"Using data for research or activities that align with ethical guidelines and provide societal benefit is permitted."}
policy.prohibitions: {"action":"shareWithoutConsent","constraint":{"name":"informedConsent","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Sharing personal data without the explicit consent of individuals is strictly prohibited."}; {"action":"exploitData","constraint":{"name":"ethicalCompliance","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Using data in a way that exploits individuals or communities, or causes harm, is prohibited."}
policy.duties: {"action":"obtainConsent","description":"Researchers and data providers must obtain informed consent from individuals before sharing their personal data."}; {"action":"protectSensitiveData","description":"Data providers are responsible for implementing appropriate measures to protect sensitive data and ensuring its responsible use."}
policy.sources: {"name":"Declaration of Helsinki (WMA)","description":"Ethical principles for research involving human subjects, including data sharing and protection.","link":"https://www.wma.net/policies-post/wma-declaration-of-helsinki-ethical-principles-for-medical-research-involving-human-subjects/"}; {"name":"GDPR Ethical Data Sharing Guidelines","description":"GDPR-specific guidance on ethical considerations for data sharing in research.","link":"https://gdpr-info.eu/"}; {"name":"EOSC Ethical Data Sharing Framework","description":"EOSC-specific recommendations for ethical data sharing and responsible handling of sensitive information.","link":"https://eosc-portal.eu/"}

policy.description: Publication and Attribution Rights define the permissions, prohibitions, and obligations involved in the publication of research outputs and proper acknowledgment of contributors. These rights ensure fairness, transparency, and adherence to ethical standards.
policy.permissions: {"action":"publishOutputs","constraint":{"name":"compliance","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"true"},"description":"Researchers are allowed to publish their outputs provided they comply with ethical and institutional guidelines."}; {"action":"acknowledgeContributions","constraint":{"name":"contributionProof","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"true"},"description":"Contributors have the right to request acknowledgment for their contributions to research outputs."}
policy.prohibitions: {"action":"plagiarism","constraint":{"name":"originality","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Plagiarism, including the uncredited use of others' work, is strictly prohibited."}; {"action":"misattributeWork","constraint":{"name":"attributionAccuracy","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Misattributing work to the wrong individual or entity is prohibited."}
policy.duties: {"action":"creditContributors","description":"Researchers must ensure that all contributors are properly credited for their roles in the research output."}; {"action":"followPublicationStandards","description":"Researchers are obligated to adhere to ethical standards and institutional policies when publishing their work."}
policy.sources: {"name":"COPE Guidelines for Publication Ethics","description":"Comprehensive guidelines for ethical publishing practices and proper attribution.","link":"https://publicationethics.org/"}; {"name":"ICMJE Authorship Guidelines","description":"International standards for determining authorship and acknowledging contributors in scientific publications.","link":"https://www.icmje.org/"}; {"name":"EOSC Publication Standards","description":"EOSC-specific recommendations for ethical publication and proper attribution in open science.","link":"https://eosc-portal.eu/"}

policy.description: Data Ownership Rights define the permissions, prohibitions, and obligations associated with establishing, maintaining, and transferring ownership of data. These rights ensure that creators and rightful owners maintain control over their datasets, promoting ethical and legal compliance in data management.
policy.permissions: {"action":"claimOwnership","constraint":{"name":"creationProof","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"true"},"description":"Data creators and rightful owners are allowed to claim ownership of their datasets, provided they can prove their creation or rights."}; {"action":"transferOwnership","constraint":{"name":"mutualAgreement","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"true"},"description":"Transferring ownership of data is allowed when all parties involved agree, and proper documentation is provided."}
policy.prohibitions: {"action":"disputeOwnershipUnjustly","constraint":{"name":"ownershipProof","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Disputing or claiming ownership of data without evidence or legal basis is prohibited."}; {"action":"violateOwnershipRights","constraint":{"name":"ownerConsent","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Using, sharing, or altering data without the explicit consent of the owner is strictly prohibited."}
policy.duties: {"action":"documentOwnership","description":"Data owners must maintain clear and verifiable records to establish their ownership rights and provide evidence when required."}; {"action":"ensureResponsibleUse","description":"Data owners must ensure their datasets are used ethically and in compliance with relevant legal and institutional guidelines."}
policy.sources: {"name":"WIPO Guidelines on Data Ownership","description":"Guidelines from the World Intellectual Property Organization on establishing and managing data ownership rights.","link":"https://www.wipo.int/"}; {"name":"EOSC Data Ownership Framework","description":"EOSC-specific recommendations for recognizing and managing data ownership in open science.","link":"https://eosc-portal.eu/"}; {"name":"OECD Guidelines on Data Governance","description":"OECD principles for managing data governance, including ownership and stewardship responsibilities.","link":"https://www.oecd.org/"}

policy.description: Digital Rights Management (DRM) Rights define the permissions, prohibitions, and obligations associated with using technical measures to control access and usage of digital content. These rights ensure compliance with licensing terms and intellectual property laws while promoting transparency and ethical practices.
policy.permissions: {"action":"implementDRMMeasures","constraint":{"name":"ownership","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"true"},"description":"Content owners are allowed to implement DRM technologies to protect their content and control access and usage."}; {"action":"enforceCompliance","constraint":{"name":"licenseCompliance","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"true"},"description":"Using DRM tools to enforce compliance with licensing terms is permitted, ensuring that users adhere to agreed usage conditions."}
policy.prohibitions: {"action":"circumventDRMMeasures","constraint":{"name":"technicalProtection","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"true"},"description":"Circumventing or disabling DRM protections is strictly prohibited and violates intellectual property laws."}; {"action":"misuseDRMTools","constraint":{"name":"ethicalUsage","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Misusing DRM tools to restrict lawful activities or exploit users is prohibited."}
policy.duties: {"action":"discloseDRMUsage","description":"Content owners must disclose the use of DRM technologies, including their purpose and impact on user rights."}; {"action":"ensureFairUse","description":"DRM measures must not interfere with lawful activities, such as fair use, accessibility requirements, or preservation efforts."}
policy.sources: {"name":"WIPO DRM Guidelines","description":"International guidelines for the use and management of DRM technologies in protecting digital content.","link":"https://www.wipo.int/"}; {"name":"EU Directive on Copyright in the Digital Single Market","description":"European Union directive outlining the legal framework for DRM and copyright compliance.","link":"https://eur-lex.europa.eu/"}; {"name":"EOSC DRM and Access Control Standards","description":"EOSC-specific recommendations for implementing DRM in open science environments.","link":"https://eosc-portal.eu/"}

policy.description: Fair Use and Fair Dealing Rights define the permissions, prohibitions, and obligations associated with the limited use of copyrighted content for purposes such as research, education, criticism, and commentary. These rights promote lawful and ethical practices under established legal frameworks.
policy.permissions: {"action":"quoteForCommentary","constraint":{"name":"portionLimit","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"smallPortion"},"description":"Quoting small portions of content for purposes such as criticism or commentary is permitted under fair use or fair dealing laws."}; {"action":"useForEducation","constraint":{"name":"educationalPurpose","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"true"},"description":"Using content for educational purposes is allowed, provided it aligns with specific legal and institutional guidelines."}
policy.prohibitions: {"action":"exceedFairUseLimits","constraint":{"name":"fairUseCriteria","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Using content beyond what is legally permissible under fair use or fair dealing principles is prohibited."}; {"action":"misattributeWork","constraint":{"name":"attribution","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Failing to credit the original author or source when using their work is prohibited."}
policy.duties: {"action":"acknowledgeSource","description":"Users must provide proper attribution to the original author or source when using their work under fair use or fair dealing."}; {"action":"assessUsage","description":"Users are obligated to evaluate whether their use aligns with fair use or fair dealing criteria, considering factors such as purpose, nature, amount, and effect."}
policy.sources: {"name":"WIPO Fair Use Guidelines","description":"International guidelines for applying fair use and fair dealing principles to copyrighted content.","link":"https://www.wipo.int/"}; {"name":"US Copyright Act (Section 107 - Fair Use)","description":"Legal framework for fair use in the United States, covering purposes such as criticism, education, and research.","link":"https://www.copyright.gov/"}; {"name":"EOSC Fair Use Principles","description":"EOSC-specific recommendations for applying fair use and fair dealing in open science environments.","link":"https://eosc-portal.eu/"}

policy.description: Accessibility Rights ensure that digital content, research outputs, and platforms are designed and delivered in a manner that is inclusive and accessible to all users, including individuals with disabilities. These rights promote compliance with accessibility standards and inclusive design principles.
policy.permissions: {"action":"adaptForAccessibility","constraint":{"name":"accessibilityCompliance","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"true"},"description":"Modifying content to meet accessibility standards, such as providing captions for videos or text alternatives for images, is permitted."}; {"action":"shareAccessibleVersions","constraint":{"name":"alternativeFormats","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"true"},"description":"Distributing alternative formats of content, such as braille, audio, or simplified text, is allowed to support users with disabilities."}
policy.prohibitions: {"action":"denyAccessibilityFeatures","constraint":{"name":"featureSupport","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Removing or disabling accessibility features, such as screen reader compatibility or captioning, is prohibited."}; {"action":"discriminateInAccess","constraint":{"name":"equalAccess","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Creating barriers or discriminating against individuals with disabilities in accessing content or platforms is strictly prohibited."}
policy.duties: {"action":"complyWithStandards","description":"Content providers must adhere to recognized accessibility standards, such as WCAG, to ensure their content is accessible to all users."}; {"action":"provideAlternativeFormats","description":"Providers are obligated to offer content in alternative formats, such as braille or audio, to accommodate diverse needs and abilities."}
policy.sources: {"name":"W3C Web Content Accessibility Guidelines (WCAG)","description":"International standards for making web content accessible to individuals with disabilities.","link":"https://www.w3.org/WAI/standards-guidelines/wcag/"}; {"name":"UN Convention on the Rights of Persons with Disabilities (CRPD)","description":"Global framework promoting the rights of persons with disabilities, including accessibility.","link":"https://www.un.org/development/desa/disabilities/convention-on-the-rights-of-persons-with-disabilities.html"}; {"name":"EOSC Accessibility Framework","description":"EOSC-specific recommendations for ensuring accessibility in open science platforms and outputs.","link":"https://eosc-portal.eu/"}

policy.description: User Privacy Rights define the permissions, prohibitions, and obligations related to the protection of personal data. These rights ensure compliance with privacy regulations, transparency in data handling practices, and safeguarding user data against misuse.
policy.permissions: {"action":"collectDataWithConsent","constraint":{"name":"userConsent","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"true"},"description":"Data collection is permitted when users provide explicit consent, ensuring they are informed about what data is being collected and why."}; {"action":"processDataForSpecificPurposes","constraint":{"name":"purposeSpecification","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"true"},"description":"Data processing is allowed only for specific purposes that have been clearly communicated to and agreed upon by users."}
policy.prohibitions: {"action":"collectDataWithoutConsent","constraint":{"name":"userConsent","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Collecting personal data without explicit consent from users is strictly prohibited."}; {"action":"shareDataWithoutAuthorization","constraint":{"name":"thirdPartyAuthorization","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Sharing user data with third parties without explicit user authorization is prohibited."}
policy.duties: {"action":"informUsers","description":"Organizations must clearly inform users about their data collection practices, the purposes of data collection, and the rights users have over their data."}; {"action":"implementSecurityMeasures","description":"Organizations are obligated to implement robust security measures to protect user data against unauthorized access, breaches, or misuse."}
policy.sources: {"name":"General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR)","description":"Comprehensive regulation governing the protection of personal data in the EU.","link":"https://gdpr-info.eu/"}; {"name":"OECD Privacy Principles","description":"International guidelines for privacy protection and responsible data handling.","link":"https://www.oecd.org/"}; {"name":"EOSC User Privacy Framework","description":"EOSC-specific recommendations for protecting user privacy in open science environments.","link":"https://eosc-portal.eu/"}

policy.description: Reproducibility and Data Integrity Rights define the permissions, prohibitions, and obligations associated with ensuring the reproducibility of research and the integrity of data. These rights support transparent, trustworthy research practices by emphasizing accurate documentation, validation, and versioning.
policy.permissions: {"action":"replicateStudies","constraint":{"name":"openData","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"true"},"description":"Replicating studies is allowed when the data and methodologies are openly shared, enabling other researchers to validate findings."}; {"action":"verifyData","constraint":{"name":"accuracyChecks","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"true"},"description":"Verifying data accuracy and consistency is permitted to ensure that research outputs are reliable and reproducible."}
policy.prohibitions: {"action":"manipulateData","constraint":{"name":"dataIntegrity","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Manipulating, altering, or fabricating data to misrepresent research findings is strictly prohibited."}; {"action":"suppressErrors","constraint":{"name":"errorTransparency","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Concealing errors or inconsistencies in data or methodologies is prohibited, as it undermines research integrity."}
policy.duties: {"action":"documentMethods","description":"Researchers must provide comprehensive documentation of methodologies, including protocols, tools, and data transformations, to enable reproducibility."}; {"action":"ensureDataIntegrity","description":"Researchers are obligated to maintain accurate, complete, and verifiable records of their data and any changes made during the research process."}
policy.sources: {"name":"OECD Principles on Research Integrity","description":"Guidelines for promoting research reproducibility and integrity in scientific studies.","link":"https://www.oecd.org/"}; {"name":"EOSC Reproducibility Guidelines","description":"EOSC-specific recommendations for ensuring reproducibility and maintaining data integrity in open science.","link":"https://eosc-portal.eu/"}; {"name":"FAIR Principles (GO FAIR Initiative)","description":"Emphasizes the importance of data reproducibility and integrity in open research ecosystems.","link":"https://www.go-fair.org/"}

policy.description: Ethical AI Usage Rights define the permissions, prohibitions, and obligations associated with deploying and using AI systems in research and digital environments. These rights promote transparency, fairness, accountability, and compliance with legal and ethical standards.
policy.permissions: {"action":"deployAIForResearch","constraint":{"name":"ethicalCompliance","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"true"},"description":"Using AI systems for research purposes is permitted, provided they operate transparently and align with ethical guidelines."}; {"action":"analyzeData","constraint":{"name":"privacyProtection","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"true"},"description":"AI systems are allowed to analyze data as long as they comply with privacy and data protection regulations."}
policy.prohibitions: {"action":"biasInAISystems","constraint":{"name":"nonDiscrimination","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Deploying AI systems with discriminatory biases or unfair practices is strictly prohibited."}; {"action":"useWithoutTransparency","constraint":{"name":"transparency","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Using AI systems without disclosing their purpose, methodology, or potential impacts is prohibited."}
policy.duties: {"action":"ensureExplainability","description":"AI systems must be explainable, with clear documentation of their processes, algorithms, and outputs to ensure accountability and trust."}; {"action":"auditForBias","description":"Regular audits must be conducted to identify, address, and mitigate biases in AI systems to ensure fair and ethical outcomes."}
policy.sources: {"name":"EU AI Act Draft","description":"Proposed legislation regulating AI usage within the European Union, focusing on ethical and accountable AI practices.","link":"https://digital-strategy.ec.europa.eu/"}; {"name":"OECD AI Principles","description":"Guidelines for ethical AI use, emphasizing transparency, accountability, and fairness.","link":"https://oecd.ai/en/"}; {"name":"EOSC AI Ethics Guidelines","description":"EOSC-specific recommendations for the ethical integration of AI in open science.","link":"https://eosc-portal.eu/"}

policy.description: Sustainable Research Practices Rights define the permissions, prohibitions, and obligations for promoting sustainability in research activities. These rights aim to ensure efficient use of resources, reduce environmental impact, and support the long-term accessibility and reuse of research outputs.
policy.permissions: {"action":"reuseResources","constraint":{"name":"efficiency","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"true"},"description":"Repurposing data, tools, and materials is permitted to minimize waste and promote resource efficiency in research."}; {"action":"shareInfrastructure","constraint":{"name":"collaboration","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"true"},"description":"Sharing computational resources and research infrastructure is allowed to foster collaboration and efficient resource utilization."}
policy.prohibitions: {"action":"wasteResources","constraint":{"name":"efficiency","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Using resources inefficiently or without regard for sustainability principles is strictly prohibited."}; {"action":"neglectEnvironmentalImpact","constraint":{"name":"impactAssessment","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Neglecting the environmental impact of research activities, including emissions and resource depletion, is prohibited."}
policy.duties: {"action":"assessEnvironmentalImpact","description":"Researchers must evaluate and minimize the environmental footprint of their activities by adopting eco-friendly practices and technologies."}; {"action":"promoteReuse","description":"Researchers are obligated to design their workflows, outputs, and resources to support reuse and long-term sustainability."}
policy.sources: {"name":"UN Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs)","description":"Global framework promoting sustainability, including targets for responsible consumption and production.","link":"https://sdgs.un.org/"}; {"name":"OECD Guidelines on Sustainable Research Practices","description":"Recommendations for integrating sustainability into research and innovation activities.","link":"https://www.oecd.org/"}; {"name":"EOSC Sustainability Framework","description":"EOSC-specific recommendations for implementing sustainable practices in open science.","link":"https://eosc-portal.eu/"}

policy.description: Collaborative Data Governance Rights define the permissions, prohibitions, and obligations associated with managing research data collaboratively. These rights ensure transparency, equitable decision-making, and compliance with legal and ethical standards.
policy.permissions: {"action":"shareGovernance","constraint":{"name":"mutualAgreement","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"true"},"description":"Sharing governance responsibilities and decision-making authority among stakeholders is permitted under mutually agreed terms."}; {"action":"accessSharedData","constraint":{"name":"governanceProtocols","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"true"},"description":"Accessing shared data is allowed when proper governance protocols are in place and all stakeholders agree."}
policy.prohibitions: {"action":"excludeStakeholders","constraint":{"name":"stakeholderInvolvement","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Excluding relevant stakeholders from data governance processes is prohibited, as it undermines transparency and equity."}; {"action":"misuseSharedData","constraint":{"name":"dataUsageAgreement","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Using shared data outside the agreed-upon terms or purposes is strictly prohibited."}
policy.duties: {"action":"defineRolesAndResponsibilities","description":"Governance structures must clearly outline the roles and responsibilities of all stakeholders involved in data management."}; {"action":"ensureTransparency","description":"Data governance processes must be transparent and equitable, ensuring all stakeholders have access to information and decision-making mechanisms."}
policy.sources: {"name":"OECD Principles on Data Governance","description":"International guidelines for collaborative and responsible data governance.","link":"https://www.oecd.org/"}; {"name":"EOSC Data Governance Framework","description":"EOSC-specific recommendations for collaboratively managing data in open science ecosystems.","link":"https://eosc-portal.eu/"}; {"name":"UN Data Governance Principles","description":"Global guidelines for fostering collaboration and equity in data governance.","link":"https://sdgs.un.org/"}

policy.description: Research Ethics and Compliance Rights define the permissions, prohibitions, and obligations necessary to ensure integrity, accountability, and adherence to ethical and legal standards in research practices.
policy.permissions: {"action":"conductEthicalResearch","constraint":{"name":"ethicsApproval","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"true"},"description":"Conducting research is allowed when it aligns with approved protocols and ethical guidelines, ensuring the welfare of subjects and integrity of the study."}; {"action":"shareFindingsTransparently","constraint":{"name":"honesty","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"true"},"description":"Sharing research findings transparently and honestly, without distortion or bias, is permitted and encouraged."}
policy.prohibitions: {"action":"fabricateResults","constraint":{"name":"dataIntegrity","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Falsifying or manipulating research findings to mislead stakeholders or distort results is strictly prohibited."}; {"action":"violateEthicalGuidelines","constraint":{"name":"ethicalCompliance","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Conducting research in ways that disregard established ethical norms or guidelines is prohibited."}
policy.duties: {"action":"ensureEthicalApproval","description":"Researchers must obtain approval from relevant ethics committees before initiating any studies involving human or animal subjects."}; {"action":"documentCompliance","description":"Researchers are obligated to maintain accurate records that demonstrate adherence to ethical and legal standards throughout the research process."}
policy.sources: {"name":"Declaration of Helsinki (WMA)","description":"Ethical principles for research involving human subjects, emphasizing informed consent and transparency.","link":"https://www.wma.net/policies-post/wma-declaration-of-helsinki-ethical-principles-for-medical-research-involving-human-subjects/"}; {"name":"OECD Research Ethics Guidelines","description":"Recommendations for promoting integrity and compliance in research practices.","link":"https://www.oecd.org/"}; {"name":"EOSC Research Ethics Framework","description":"EOSC-specific guidance on ensuring ethical and compliant research in open science.","link":"https://eosc-portal.eu/"}

policy.description: Long-Term Data Stewardship Rights define the permissions, prohibitions, and obligations necessary for preserving, managing, and ensuring the usability of research data for future generations. These rights emphasize sustainability, adherence to archival standards, and long-term accessibility.
policy.permissions: {"action":"preserveData","constraint":{"name":"repositoryApproval","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"true"},"description":"Archiving research data for long-term use is permitted for institutions and repositories that meet preservation standards."}; {"action":"migrateFormats","constraint":{"name":"formatCompatibility","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"true"},"description":"Converting data to newer formats is allowed to maintain its accessibility and usability over time."}
policy.prohibitions: {"action":"neglectMaintenance","constraint":{"name":"dataManagement","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Failing to maintain archived data, leading to its degradation or loss, is strictly prohibited."}; {"action":"restrictFutureAccess","constraint":{"name":"accessibility","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Imposing unnecessary restrictions that hinder the future accessibility of archived data is prohibited."}
policy.duties: {"action":"ensureDataIntegrity","description":"Repositories must implement robust measures to protect archived data from corruption, loss, and unauthorized access."}; {"action":"adoptPreservationStandards","description":"Institutions are obligated to follow recognized standards for data archiving and stewardship, ensuring sustainability and usability."}
policy.sources: {"name":"FAIR Principles (GO FAIR Initiative)","description":"Guidelines emphasizing the importance of long-term data stewardship to ensure data is Findable, Accessible, Interoperable, and Reusable.","link":"https://www.go-fair.org/"}; {"name":"OECD Guidelines on Digital Preservation","description":"Recommendations for sustainable data stewardship and long-term digital preservation.","link":"https://www.oecd.org/"}; {"name":"EOSC Long-Term Data Stewardship Framework","description":"EOSC-specific guidance for ensuring sustainable data preservation and accessibility.","link":"https://eosc-portal.eu/"}

policy.description: Public Engagement and Outreach Rights define the permissions, prohibitions, and obligations associated with involving the public in research and sharing scientific knowledge. These rights promote accessibility, inclusivity, and ethical communication.
policy.permissions: {"action":"shareFindings","constraint":{"name":"ethicalCommunication","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"true"},"description":"Sharing research findings with the public through forums, media, and educational events is permitted, provided the communication is ethical and accurate."}; {"action":"collaborateWithCommunities","constraint":{"name":"inclusiveEngagement","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"true"},"description":"Involving public groups and communities in research projects is allowed, promoting collaboration and mutual understanding."}
policy.prohibitions: {"action":"spreadMisinformation","constraint":{"name":"communicationAccuracy","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Disseminating false or misleading information to the public is strictly prohibited."}; {"action":"excludeUnderservedGroups","constraint":{"name":"engagementEquity","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Restricting public engagement to privileged or easily accessible groups, thereby excluding underserved communities, is prohibited."}
policy.duties: {"action":"useInclusiveLanguage","description":"Researchers must communicate in a manner that is understandable and accessible to diverse audiences, avoiding technical jargon when unnecessary."}; {"action":"promoteTransparency","description":"Researchers are obligated to ensure their public communication is accurate, transparent, and aligns with ethical standards."}
policy.sources: {"name":"UNESCO Science Communication Guidelines","description":"Recommendations for ethical and effective communication of scientific knowledge to the public.","link":"https://www.unesco.org/"}; {"name":"OECD Public Engagement in Science Policy","description":"Guidelines for involving the public in science and promoting inclusive engagement practices.","link":"https://www.oecd.org/"}; {"name":"EOSC Public Engagement Framework","description":"EOSC-specific recommendations for public outreach and engagement in open science.","link":"https://eosc-portal.eu/"}

policy.description: Data Ethics and Fairness Rights define the permissions, prohibitions, and obligations associated with ensuring ethical and fair practices in data collection, usage, and sharing. These rights promote equity, transparency, and adherence to ethical guidelines.
policy.permissions: {"action":"collectDataTransparently","constraint":{"name":"informedConsent","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"true"},"description":"Collecting data is allowed when the processes are transparent, and explicit consent is obtained from the individuals involved."}; {"action":"useDataEquitably","constraint":{"name":"nonExploitation","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"true"},"description":"Using data for purposes that promote fairness and avoid exploiting or disadvantaging individuals or groups is permitted."}
policy.prohibitions: {"action":"discriminateWithData","constraint":{"name":"biasMitigation","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Using data in ways that perpetuate bias or discrimination is strictly prohibited."}; {"action":"collectDataCovertly","constraint":{"name":"transparency","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Collecting data without informed consent or transparency is prohibited and violates ethical standards."}
policy.duties: {"action":"ensureDataFairness","description":"Researchers must evaluate and mitigate biases in data collection and usage, ensuring fairness and equity."}; {"action":"protectVulnerableGroups","description":"Researchers must take extra precautions to safeguard the rights and interests of vulnerable populations, ensuring their data is handled ethically."}
policy.sources: {"name":"OECD Data Ethics Framework","description":"International guidelines for ethical and fair data practices.","link":"https://www.oecd.org/"}; {"name":"GDPR Ethical Data Handling Principles","description":"Guidelines emphasizing fairness and transparency in personal data handling under GDPR.","link":"https://gdpr-info.eu/"}; {"name":"EOSC Data Ethics Framework","description":"EOSC-specific recommendations for ethical and fair data practices in open science.","link":"https://eosc-portal.eu/"}

policy.description: Copyright Exceptions and Limitations Rights define the permissions, prohibitions, and obligations for the limited use of copyrighted material without explicit permission. These rights include exceptions for purposes such as education, research, and criticism, promoting fair and lawful practices.
policy.permissions: {"action":"useForEducation","constraint":{"name":"nonCommercialPurpose","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"true"},"description":"Using copyrighted material for non-commercial educational purposes is permitted, provided it complies with specific conditions set by law."}; {"action":"quoteForCriticismOrCommentary","constraint":{"name":"reasonablePortion","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"true"},"description":"Quoting copyrighted material for criticism or commentary is allowed when properly attributed and within reasonable limits."}
policy.prohibitions: {"action":"exceedLimitations","constraint":{"name":"legalCompliance","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Using copyrighted material beyond what is legally permitted under exceptions and limitations is strictly prohibited."}; {"action":"failToAttribute","constraint":{"name":"attribution","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Failing to provide proper attribution for copyrighted material, even when relying on exceptions, is prohibited."}
policy.duties: {"action":"respectLimitations","description":"Users must ensure their use of copyrighted material stays within the boundaries defined by exceptions and limitations."}; {"action":"provideAttribution","description":"Users are obligated to credit the original creator when utilizing copyrighted material under exceptions and limitations."}
policy.sources: {"name":"WIPO Guide to Copyright Exceptions","description":"Guidelines for understanding and applying copyright exceptions under international frameworks.","link":"https://www.wipo.int/"}; {"name":"US Copyright Act (Fair Use, Section 107)","description":"Legal framework for exceptions and limitations under fair use principles in the United States.","link":"https://www.copyright.gov/"}; {"name":"EU Copyright Directive","description":"European Union directive outlining permissible exceptions and limitations to copyright laws.","link":"https://eur-lex.europa.eu/"}

policy.description: Research Data Sharing Rights define the permissions, prohibitions, and obligations for sharing research data. These rights emphasize transparency, collaboration, and adherence to ethical and legal standards while protecting sensitive information.
policy.permissions: {"action":"shareOpenData","constraint":{"name":"openAccessCompliance","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"true"},"description":"Sharing non-sensitive research data that complies with open access and ethical guidelines is permitted to promote transparency and collaboration."}; {"action":"collaborateOnDataSharing","constraint":{"name":"dataSharingAgreement","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"true"},"description":"Collaborating on data sharing is allowed when agreements are in place to define roles, responsibilities, and terms."}
policy.prohibitions: {"action":"shareWithoutAuthorization","constraint":{"name":"authorization","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Sharing research data without explicit permissions or agreements is prohibited."}; {"action":"compromisePrivacy","constraint":{"name":"privacyProtection","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Sharing data that violates privacy or confidentiality agreements is strictly prohibited."}
policy.duties: {"action":"ensureDataAnonymization","description":"Researchers must anonymize sensitive data before sharing to protect individual identities and comply with privacy standards."}; {"action":"documentDataSharingAgreements","description":"All data-sharing activities must be formalized with clear agreements that specify conditions, responsibilities, and intended use."}
policy.sources: {"name":"FAIR Principles (GO FAIR Initiative)","description":"Guidelines emphasizing data sharing that is Findable, Accessible, Interoperable, and Reusable.","link":"https://www.go-fair.org/"}; {"name":"OECD Guidelines for Research Data Sharing","description":"International guidelines for responsible and transparent data sharing.","link":"https://www.oecd.org/"}; {"name":"EOSC Data Sharing Framework","description":"EOSC-specific recommendations for facilitating responsible research data sharing.","link":"https://eosc-portal.eu/"}

policy.description: Data Security and Risk Mitigation Rights define the permissions, prohibitions, and obligations for ensuring the security of research data. These rights emphasize proactive measures, compliance with legal standards, and safeguarding sensitive information from unauthorized access, breaches, or corruption.
policy.permissions: {"action":"encryptData","constraint":{"name":"encryptionStandards","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"true"},"description":"Using encryption methods to protect sensitive data during storage and transmission is permitted and encouraged to ensure confidentiality."}; {"action":"controlAccess","constraint":{"name":"accessAuthorization","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"true"},"description":"Implementing access controls to restrict data access to authorized personnel is permitted, ensuring data security and compliance."}
policy.prohibitions: {"action":"neglectSecurityMeasures","constraint":{"name":"securityCompliance","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Storing or sharing data without adequate security protections, such as encryption or access controls, is prohibited."}; {"action":"shareSecurityCredentials","constraint":{"name":"authorizedPersonnel","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Sharing access credentials or encryption keys with unauthorized individuals is strictly prohibited."}
policy.duties: {"action":"monitorDataSecurity","description":"Organizations must continuously monitor and audit their data security measures to identify and address vulnerabilities proactively."}; {"action":"implementIncidentResponsePlans","description":"Researchers and institutions must establish and maintain protocols for responding to data breaches or security incidents promptly and effectively."}
policy.sources: {"name":"ISO/IEC 27001: Information Security Management","description":"International standard for implementing and maintaining information security management systems.","link":"https://www.iso.org/"}; {"name":"OECD Data Security Guidelines","description":"Recommendations for safeguarding research data against risks and breaches.","link":"https://www.oecd.org/"}; {"name":"EOSC Data Security Framework","description":"EOSC-specific guidance for ensuring data security in open science environments.","link":"https://eosc-portal.eu/"}

policy.description: Research Data Provenance Rights define the permissions, prohibitions, and obligations for maintaining and verifying the provenance of research data. These rights ensure transparency, accountability, and reproducibility by tracking the origin, transformations, and ownership of data.
policy.permissions: {"action":"trackDataProvenance","constraint":{"name":"provenanceTools","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"true"},"description":"Using tools and methods to document the origin and history of research data is permitted to ensure transparency and accountability."}; {"action":"shareProvenanceMetadata","constraint":{"name":"collaborativeUse","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"true"},"description":"Sharing metadata about data provenance with collaborators and repositories is allowed to promote transparency and reproducibility."}
policy.prohibitions: {"action":"omitProvenanceInformation","constraint":{"name":"provenanceCompleteness","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Omitting or falsifying information about the origin or transformations of data is strictly prohibited."}; {"action":"obfuscateDataSources","constraint":{"name":"sourceTransparency","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Obscuring or misrepresenting the sources of data is prohibited, as it undermines trust and accountability."}
policy.duties: {"action":"maintainProvenanceRecords","description":"Researchers must maintain detailed and accurate records of data provenance, including origins, transformations, and ownership."}; {"action":"verifyProvenance","description":"Researchers must verify the authenticity and reliability of data sources and associated documentation to ensure research integrity."}
policy.sources: {"name":"W3C PROV Data Model","description":"Standards for documenting and exchanging provenance information for data and research workflows.","link":"https://www.w3.org/TR/prov-overview/"}; {"name":"OECD Guidelines on Research Transparency","description":"International guidelines promoting the documentation of research data provenance to ensure transparency and reproducibility.","link":"https://www.oecd.org/"}; {"name":"EOSC Data Provenance Framework","description":"EOSC-specific guidance for ensuring the provenance of research data in open science environments.","link":"https://eosc-portal.eu/"}

policy.description: Ethical Research Collaboration Rights define the permissions, prohibitions, and obligations for ensuring fairness, transparency, and mutual respect in collaborative research. These rights emphasize adherence to ethical and legal standards to foster productive and inclusive partnerships.
policy.permissions: {"action":"collaborateEquitably","constraint":{"name":"roleClarity","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"true"},"description":"Collaborating equitably is permitted when roles and contributions are clearly defined and mutually agreed upon by all parties."}; {"action":"shareResponsibilities","constraint":{"name":"expertiseAlignment","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"true"},"description":"Distributing responsibilities among collaborators based on their expertise and capacity is allowed to promote efficiency and fairness."}
policy.prohibitions: {"action":"exploitCollaborators","constraint":{"name":"fairness","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Exploiting the work, resources, or contributions of collaborators unfairly is strictly prohibited."}; {"action":"misattributeContributions","constraint":{"name":"creditAttribution","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Failing to credit or misattributing the contributions of collaborators is prohibited, as it violates ethical principles."}
policy.duties: {"action":"establishAgreements","description":"Researchers must formalize collaboration agreements that define roles, responsibilities, and expectations to prevent conflicts and ensure transparency."}; {"action":"promoteInclusivity","description":"Collaborative research efforts must prioritize inclusivity and ensure equal opportunities for all participants, regardless of their background."}
policy.sources: {"name":"ICMJE Guidelines for Authorship and Collaboration","description":"International standards for determining authorship and fostering ethical collaboration.","link":"https://www.icmje.org/"}; {"name":"OECD Guidelines on Research Collaboration","description":"Recommendations for promoting fairness, transparency, and accountability in collaborative research.","link":"https://www.oecd.org/"}; {"name":"EOSC Collaboration Framework","description":"EOSC-specific guidance for fostering ethical and inclusive research collaborations.","link":"https://eosc-portal.eu/"}

policy.description: Open Access Publication Rights define the permissions, prohibitions, and obligations for making research publications freely accessible. These rights promote transparency, accessibility, and equitable knowledge sharing while adhering to ethical standards and open access mandates.
policy.permissions: {"action":"publishOpenly","constraint":{"name":"openAccessLicense","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"true"},"description":"Making research publications freely available under open access licenses is permitted, promoting global accessibility and knowledge sharing."}; {"action":"reusePublishedContent","constraint":{"name":"attribution","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"true"},"description":"Reusing content published under open access licenses for non-commercial purposes is allowed, provided proper attribution is given."}
policy.prohibitions: {"action":"restrictAccess","constraint":{"name":"accessibility","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Imposing unnecessary paywalls or restrictions on openly published work is prohibited."}; {"action":"violateOpenAccessTerms","constraint":{"name":"licenseCompliance","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Reusing or sharing open access content in ways that breach the terms of its license is strictly prohibited."}
policy.duties: {"action":"useOpenAccessLicenses","description":"Researchers must apply appropriate open access licenses to their publications to clarify reuse rights and ensure compliance."}; {"action":"complyWithFundersPolicies","description":"Researchers are obligated to adhere to the open access requirements of their funding agencies or institutions."}
