policy.sources: {"name":"Plan S Guidelines","description":"Guidelines from the Coalition S initiative for implementing open access publishing requirements.","link":"https://www.coalition-s.org/"}; {"name":"UNESCO Open Science Recommendations","description":"Global framework for promoting open access in scientific research and publications.","link":"https://www.unesco.org/"}; {"name":"EOSC Open Access Framework","description":"EOSC-specific recommendations for fostering open access to research outputs in open science environments.","link":"https://eosc-portal.eu/"}

policy.description: Intellectual Property Licensing Rights define the permissions, prohibitions, and obligations for licensing intellectual property. These rights promote proper usage, distribution, and protection of creators’ rights while ensuring compliance with licensing terms.
policy.permissions: {"action":"licenseForNonCommercialUse","constraint":{"name":"nonCommercialPurpose","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"true"},"description":"Licensing intellectual property for non-commercial purposes is permitted under conditions specified by the licensor."}; {"action":"distributeUnderOpenLicenses","constraint":{"name":"openLicense","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"true"},"description":"Distributing intellectual property under open or permissive licenses, such as Creative Commons, is allowed to promote accessibility."}
policy.prohibitions: {"action":"violateLicenseTerms","constraint":{"name":"licenseCompliance","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Using or distributing intellectual property in ways that breach the terms of its license is strictly prohibited."}; {"action":"revokeWithoutCause","constraint":{"name":"agreementAdherence","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Revoking licenses without cause or failing to adhere to the agreed terms and conditions is prohibited."}
policy.duties: {"action":"defineLicenseTermsClearly","description":"Licensors must clearly define the terms and conditions of their licenses to avoid ambiguities and disputes."}; {"action":"monitorCompliance","description":"Licensors are obligated to monitor and enforce compliance with the terms of their intellectual property licenses."}
policy.sources: {"name":"Creative Commons Licensing Framework","description":"A widely used framework for licensing creative works, enabling clear usage terms.","link":"https://creativecommons.org/"}; {"name":"WIPO Guide on Licensing IP Rights","description":"International guidelines for licensing intellectual property rights.","link":"https://www.wipo.int/"}; {"name":"EOSC Licensing Framework","description":"EOSC-specific guidance for licensing research outputs and ensuring compliance with open science principles.","link":"https://eosc-portal.eu/"}

policy.description: Data Collaboration and Sharing Agreements define the permissions, prohibitions, and obligations for establishing clear and transparent terms for data sharing and collaboration. These agreements ensure compliance with legal and ethical standards while promoting equitable partnerships.
policy.permissions: {"action":"shareDataWithAgreements","constraint":{"name":"mutualAgreement","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"true"},"description":"Sharing data with collaborators is allowed when there is a formal agreement specifying roles, responsibilities, and usage terms."}; {"action":"establishJointOwnership","constraint":{"name":"ownershipAgreement","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"true"},"description":"Defining joint ownership of collaboratively generated data is permitted under mutually agreed terms."}
policy.prohibitions: {"action":"shareWithoutAgreement","constraint":{"name":"formalAgreement","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Sharing data without a formal agreement outlining roles, responsibilities, and usage terms is strictly prohibited."}; {"action":"misuseSharedData","constraint":{"name":"authorizedUse","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Using shared data for purposes not covered by the agreement is prohibited and undermines trust and compliance."}
policy.duties: {"action":"documentAgreements","description":"Researchers and institutions must formalize collaboration and data sharing agreements in writing to ensure clarity and accountability."}; {"action":"ensureCompliance","description":"All parties are obligated to adhere to the terms and conditions specified in the agreement, promoting trust and ethical collaboration."}
policy.sources: {"name":"OECD Guidelines on Data Sharing Agreements","description":"International guidelines for creating clear and equitable data sharing agreements.","link":"https://www.oecd.org/"}; {"name":"EOSC Data Collaboration Framework","description":"EOSC-specific guidance for facilitating effective and ethical data sharing agreements.","link":"https://eosc-portal.eu/"}; {"name":"WIPO Best Practices for Data Sharing","description":"Recommendations for creating agreements that promote transparency and protect data rights.","link":"https://www.wipo.int/"}

policy.description: Data Curation and Management Responsibilities define the permissions, prohibitions, and obligations for ensuring that research data remains accurate, organized, and accessible throughout its lifecycle. These responsibilities promote adherence to best practices, compliance with standards, and long-term usability.
policy.permissions: {"action":"organizeData","constraint":{"name":"usabilityEnhancement","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"true"},"description":"Structuring and formatting data to enhance its accessibility and usability is permitted and encouraged as part of good data management practices."}; {"action":"archiveData","constraint":{"name":"secureRepository","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"true"},"description":"Storing data in secure repositories for long-term preservation and potential reuse is permitted under proper conditions."}
policy.prohibitions: {"action":"neglectMaintenance","constraint":{"name":"dataIntegrity","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Leaving curated data unmaintained, resulting in its degradation or loss, is strictly prohibited."}; {"action":"storeWithoutMetadata","constraint":{"name":"metadataCompliance","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Archiving data without proper metadata that describes its content, context, and provenance is prohibited."}
policy.duties: {"action":"ensureDataQuality","description":"Researchers must perform regular checks to ensure that curated data maintains accuracy, consistency, and integrity."}; {"action":"provideMetadata","description":"Researchers are obligated to provide comprehensive metadata for curated data, ensuring it is well-documented and reusable."}
policy.sources: {"name":"FAIR Principles for Data Management","description":"Guidelines for making data Findable, Accessible, Interoperable, and Reusable through effective curation.","link":"https://www.go-fair.org/"}; {"name":"OECD Guidelines on Data Curation","description":"Recommendations for ensuring effective and sustainable data management practices.","link":"https://www.oecd.org/"}; {"name":"EOSC Data Curation Framework","description":"EOSC-specific guidance for the curation and management of research data in open science.","link":"https://eosc-portal.eu/"}

policy.description: Digital Preservation and Accessibility Standards define the permissions, prohibitions, and obligations for ensuring the long-term usability and accessibility of digital content and research outputs. These standards emphasize sustainability, compliance with recognized frameworks, and proactive maintenance.
policy.permissions: {"action":"migrateContent","constraint":{"name":"formatCompatibility","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"true"},"description":"Converting digital content to newer formats to maintain its accessibility over time is permitted and encouraged."}; {"action":"utilizePreservationStandards","constraint":{"name":"recognizedFrameworks","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"true"},"description":"Implementing recognized standards and frameworks, such as OAIS, for digital preservation is permitted to ensure long-term usability."}
policy.prohibitions: {"action":"neglectAccessibility","constraint":{"name":"repositoryStandards","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Storing digital content in formats or repositories that hinder future accessibility is strictly prohibited."}; {"action":"failToBackUp","constraint":{"name":"backupSchedule","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Failing to perform regular backups of preserved content, increasing the risk of data loss, is prohibited."}
policy.duties: {"action":"ensureUsabilityOverTime","description":"Researchers and institutions must ensure that preserved digital content remains usable as technologies and formats evolve."}; {"action":"adoptSustainablePractices","description":"Institutions are obligated to implement sustainable technologies and methods to ensure long-term preservation and accessibility of digital content."}
policy.sources: {"name":"ISO 14721: Open Archival Information System (OAIS)","description":"An international standard for long-term digital preservation frameworks.","link":"https://www.iso.org/"}; {"name":"UNESCO Guidelines on Digital Preservation","description":"Recommendations for the preservation of digital heritage and ensuring long-term access.","link":"https://www.unesco.org/"}; {"name":"EOSC Preservation and Accessibility Framework","description":"EOSC-specific guidance for preserving and ensuring the accessibility of digital research outputs.","link":"https://eosc-portal.eu/"}

policy.description: Compliance with Open Science Policies defines the permissions, prohibitions, and obligations for ensuring adherence to institutional, national, and international guidelines for open science. These policies promote transparency, accessibility, and collaboration in research practices.

policy.prohibitions: {"action":"restrictOpenAccess","constraint":{"name":"policyCompliance","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Imposing barriers or restrictions that violate open science policies is strictly prohibited."}; {"action":"failToComplyWithFundersMandates","constraint":{"name":"funderRequirements","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Ignoring open science requirements set by funding agencies is prohibited and may result in penalties."}
policy.duties: {"action":"adoptOpenSciencePractices","description":"Researchers must adopt practices that align with open science principles, such as sharing data, tools, and results openly."}; {"action":"documentCompliance","description":"Researchers are obligated to document their adherence to open science policies in project reports and publications to demonstrate compliance."}
policy.sources: {"name":"UNESCO Open Science Recommendations","description":"International guidelines for fostering transparency and collaboration in scientific research.","link":"https://www.unesco.org/"}; {"name":"European Open Science Cloud (EOSC) Policies","description":"EOSC-specific policies for promoting open science and ensuring compliance with its principles.","link":"https://eosc-portal.eu/"}; {"name":"Plan S and cOAlition S Guidelines","description":"Frameworks for open access publishing and compliance with funders' open science mandates.","link":"https://www.coalition-s.org/"}

policy.description: Research Misconduct Prevention Rights define the permissions, prohibitions, and obligations necessary to prevent unethical practices in research. These rights promote integrity, accountability, and adherence to ethical guidelines.

policy.prohibitions: {"action":"engageInMisconduct","constraint":{"name":"ethicalCompliance","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Engaging in research misconduct, such as fabrication, falsification, or plagiarism, is strictly prohibited."}; {"action":"retaliateAgainstWhistleblowers","constraint":{"name":"whistleblowerProtection","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"true"},"description":"Retaliating against individuals who report research misconduct in good faith is prohibited."}
policy.duties: {"action":"promoteEthicalResearchCulture","description":"Institutions must foster a culture that prioritizes ethical research practices and accountability among researchers."}; {"action":"establishMisconductPolicies","description":"Institutions are obligated to implement clear policies and procedures for addressing allegations of research misconduct effectively."}
policy.sources: {"name":"Singapore Statement on Research Integrity","description":"An international framework promoting integrity in research and providing guidelines for responsible conduct.","link":"https://wcrif.org/guidance/singapore-statement"}; {"name":"OECD Best Practices for Research Integrity","description":"Guidelines for fostering integrity and addressing research misconduct.","link":"https://www.oecd.org/"}; {"name":"EOSC Research Integrity Framework","description":"EOSC-specific recommendations for promoting integrity and preventing misconduct in open science.","link":"https://eosc-portal.eu/"}

policy.description: Data Interoperability and Integration Rights define the permissions, prohibitions, and obligations for ensuring that research data can be seamlessly integrated across systems and platforms. These rights emphasize the use of standardized protocols, metadata, and tools to promote collaboration and usability.
policy.permissions: {"action":"useStandardizedFormats","constraint":{"name":"formatCompliance","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"true"},"description":"Adopting and using standardized data formats is permitted to ensure compatibility and facilitate integration."}; {"action":"integrateDataFromMultipleSources","constraint":{"name":"integrationApproval","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"true"},"description":"Combining data from various systems and platforms for collaborative projects is allowed when compatibility is maintained."}
policy.prohibitions: {"action":"ignoreStandards","constraint":{"name":"standardCompliance","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Storing or sharing data in non-standardized formats that hinder interoperability and integration is strictly prohibited."}; {"action":"compromiseDataIntegrityDuringIntegration","constraint":{"name":"dataIntegrity","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Altering or misrepresenting data during the integration process is prohibited, as it undermines trust and reliability."}
policy.duties: {"action":"adoptMetadataStandards","description":"Researchers must use standardized metadata formats to ensure data can be discovered and integrated across systems."}; {"action":"ensureCompatibility","description":"Institutions are obligated to ensure that data is compatible with commonly used tools, systems, and platforms for interoperability."}
policy.sources: {"name":"FAIR Principles (GO FAIR Initiative)","description":"Guidelines promoting Findable, Accessible, Interoperable, and Reusable data to support integration.","link":"https://www.go-fair.org/"}; {"name":"W3C Standards for Data Interoperability","description":"Recommendations for ensuring data interoperability across web and research platforms.","link":"https://www.w3.org/"}; {"name":"EOSC Data Interoperability Framework","description":"EOSC-specific guidance for promoting interoperability and seamless integration of research data.","link":"https://eosc-portal.eu/"}

policy.prohibitions: {"action":"biasInAIModels","constraint":{"name":"fairnessCompliance","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Developing or deploying AI systems that introduce or perpetuate bias is strictly prohibited."}; {"action":"misuseAI","constraint":{"name":"ethicalUse","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Using AI for deceptive or harmful purposes in research is prohibited."}
policy.duties: {"action":"ensureExplainability","description":"Researchers must ensure that AI models and tools are explainable and understandable to relevant stakeholders, fostering trust and transparency."}; {"action":"auditForCompliance","description":"Regular audits must be conducted to ensure that AI systems comply with ethical and legal standards, addressing potential risks and biases."}
policy.sources: {"name":"OECD AI Principles","description":"Guidelines for the ethical and responsible use of AI, emphasizing transparency, accountability, and inclusivity.","link":"https://www.oecd.org/"}; {"name":"EU AI Act Draft","description":"Proposed legislation regulating AI usage within the European Union, focusing on ethical AI development and deployment.","link":"https://digital-strategy.ec.europa.eu/"}; {"name":"EOSC Responsible AI Framework","description":"EOSC-specific guidance for ensuring the ethical use of AI in open science.","link":"https://eosc-portal.eu/"}

policy.description: Collaborative Open Science Projects Rights define the permissions, prohibitions, and obligations for participating in and supporting collaborative open science initiatives. policy.prohibitions: {"action":"excludeStakeholdersWithoutCause","constraint":{"name":"stakeholderEquity","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Excluding relevant contributors or stakeholders from collaborations without valid reasons is prohibited."}; {"action":"restrictSharingOfOpenResources","constraint":{"name":"openCollaboration","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Imposing restrictions on resources designated for open collaboration is prohibited."}
policy.duties: {"action":"establishClearAgreements","description":"Collaborators must formalize agreements outlining roles, responsibilities, and expectations to avoid conflicts and ensure transparency."}; {"action":"promoteEquityAndInclusivity","description":"Projects must ensure equal opportunities and inclusivity for all participants, fostering diverse contributions and perspectives."}
policy.sources: {"name":"UNESCO Open Science Guidelines","description":"International recommendations for fostering collaboration and inclusivity in open science initiatives.","link":"https://www.unesco.org/"}; {"name":"EOSC Collaboration and Open Science Framework","description":"EOSC-specific guidance for supporting and managing collaborative open science projects.","link":"https://eosc-portal.eu/"}; {"name":"OECD Principles for Research Collaboration","description":"Guidelines for promoting fairness, transparency, and equity in collaborative research practices.","link":"https://www.oecd.org/"}

policy.description: Ethical Data Governance in Open Science Rights define the permissions, prohibitions, and obligations for managing research data ethically in open science environments. These rights emphasize accountability, transparency, inclusivity, and compliance with legal and ethical standards.
policy.permissions: {"action":"shareGovernedData","constraint":{"name":"ethicalFramework","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"true"},"description":"Sharing research data under clear governance frameworks and ethical guidelines is permitted to promote transparency and collaboration."}; {"action":"implementAccessControls","constraint":{"name":"privacyCompliance","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"true"},"description":"Setting access controls for sensitive data to ensure compliance with privacy standards is allowed."}
policy.prohibitions: {"action":"compromiseDataPrivacy","constraint":{"name":"privacyProtection","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Sharing or using data in ways that violate privacy agreements or ethical guidelines is strictly prohibited."}; {"action":"excludeKeyStakeholders","constraint":{"name":"stakeholderInclusivity","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Excluding stakeholders from data governance processes without valid justification is prohibited."}
policy.duties: {"action":"ensureAccountability","description":"Institutions and researchers must ensure that data governance processes are transparent, accountable, and compliant with legal and ethical standards."}; {"action":"adoptInclusivePolicies","description":"Data governance frameworks must promote inclusivity and equitable participation of all stakeholders involved in open science."}
policy.sources: {"name":"OECD Guidelines on Data Governance","description":"Recommendations for promoting ethical and effective data governance practices.","link":"https://www.oecd.org/"}; {"name":"FAIR Principles for Data Governance","description":"Guidelines for implementing data governance practices that align with the principles of Findability, Accessibility, Interoperability, and Reusability.","link":"https://www.go-fair.org/"}; {"name":"EOSC Ethical Data Governance Framework","description":"EOSC-specific guidance for ethical data governance in open science environments.","link":"https://eosc-portal.eu/"}

policy.description: Transparency in Research Funding and Outputs Rights define the permissions, prohibitions, and obligations for ensuring openness and accountability in research funding sources and the dissemination of research outputs. These rights promote trust, credibility, and adherence to ethical standards.

policy.prohibitions: {"action":"concealFundingSources","constraint":{"name":"disclosureRequirement","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Withholding information about research funding sources is prohibited and undermines transparency and credibility."}; {"action":"misrepresentResearchOutputs","constraint":{"name":"accuracyRequirement","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Presenting research outputs in a misleading or deceptive manner is strictly prohibited."}
policy.duties: {"action":"provideFundingTransparency","description":"Researchers must disclose all sources of funding and any potential conflicts of interest to ensure transparency and trust."}; {"action":"ensureOpenAccessToOutputs","description":"Researchers are obligated to make their research outputs available in line with open access policies and agreements to promote public accessibility."}
policy.sources: {"name":"OECD Guidelines on Research Transparency","description":"International recommendations for ensuring transparency in research funding and dissemination.","link":"https://www.oecd.org/"}; {"name":"UNESCO Open Science Recommendations","description":"Global framework for fostering openness and transparency in scientific research.","link":"https://www.unesco.org/"}; {"name":"EOSC Transparency Framework","description":"EOSC-specific guidance for promoting transparency in research funding and outputs in open science.","link":"https://eosc-portal.eu/"}

policy.description: Privacy and Data Protection Rights define the permissions, prohibitions, and obligations for safeguarding personal data in research. These rights emphasize compliance with legal frameworks, ethical standards, and the implementation of safeguards to protect sensitive information.

policy.prohibitions: {"action":"shareDataWithoutConsent","constraint":{"name":"authorization","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Sharing personal data without explicit consent or legal authorization is strictly prohibited."}; {"action":"storeDataInsecurely","constraint":{"name":"securityCompliance","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Storing personal data in ways that compromise its security is prohibited."}
policy.duties: {"action":"implementSecurityMeasures","description":"Researchers must implement appropriate security measures, such as encryption and access controls, to safeguard personal data."}; {"action":"complyWithDataProtectionLaws","description":"Researchers are obligated to adhere to applicable laws, such as GDPR, to ensure the lawful and ethical processing of personal data."}
policy.sources: {"name":"General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR)","description":"European Union regulation outlining requirements for the processing and protection of personal data.","link":"https://gdpr-info.eu/"}; {"name":"OECD Guidelines on Privacy and Data Protection","description":"Recommendations for ensuring the privacy and protection of personal data in research.","link":"https://www.oecd.org/"}; {"name":"EOSC Data Protection Framework","description":"EOSC-specific guidance for compliance with privacy and data protection requirements in open science.","link":"https://eosc-portal.eu/"}

policy.description: Ethical Review and Approval Processes Rights define the permissions, prohibitions, and obligations for conducting ethical reviews and obtaining approval for research projects. These rights emphasize adherence to ethical standards, transparency, and accountability to protect participants and ensure integrity.
policy.permissions: {"action":"submitResearchForEthicalReview","constraint":{"name":"ethicsCommittee","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"true"},"description":"Submitting research proposals to ethics committees for evaluation is permitted to ensure adherence to ethical standards."}; {"action":"implementApprovedProtocols","constraint":{"name":"protocolCompliance","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"true"},"description":"Conducting research in accordance with approved ethical protocols is permitted and encouraged."}
policy.prohibitions: {"action":"bypassEthicalReview","constraint":{"name":"reviewRequirement","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Conducting research without undergoing an ethical review process is strictly prohibited."}; {"action":"deviateFromApprovedProtocols","constraint":{"name":"additionalApproval","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Making changes to research protocols without obtaining additional approval is prohibited."}
policy.duties: {"action":"ensureEthicalCompliance","description":"Researchers must comply with the guidelines and decisions of ethical review boards to uphold integrity and protect participants."}; {"action":"documentReviewOutcomes","description":"Researchers are obligated to maintain records of ethical reviews and approvals as part of the project documentation."}
policy.sources: {"name":"Declaration of Helsinki","description":"International ethical principles for research involving human subjects.","link":"https://www.wma.net/"}; {"name":"OECD Guidelines on Ethical Research Review","description":"Recommendations for implementing effective and transparent ethical review processes.","link":"https://www.oecd.org/"}; {"name":"EOSC Ethical Review Framework","description":"EOSC-specific guidance for conducting and documenting ethical reviews in open science research.","link":"https://eosc-portal.eu/"}

policy.description: Research Reporting and Accountability Rights define the permissions, prohibitions, and obligations for transparent, accurate, and accountable reporting of research activities and outcomes. These rights promote ethical practices, reliability, and reproducibility in research reporting.
policy.permissions: {"action":"publishResearchFindings","constraint":{"name":"transparencyCompliance","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"true"},"description":"Publishing research findings in an accurate and transparent manner is permitted to ensure trust and credibility."}; {"action":"useStandardReportingFormats","constraint":{"name":"standardAdherence","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"true"},"description":"Adopting standardized formats and guidelines for research reporting is permitted to promote clarity and consistency."}
policy.prohibitions: {"action":"misreportResults","constraint":{"name":"dataIntegrity","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Manipulating or falsifying research data or findings is strictly prohibited and undermines credibility."}; {"action":"omitKeyInformation","constraint":{"name":"completeness","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Excluding critical details that could affect the interpretation of results is prohibited."}
policy.duties: {"action":"ensureAccuracy","description":"Researchers must ensure that all reported data and findings are accurate, verifiable, and consistent with ethical standards."}; {"action":"provideReproducibleResults","description":"Researchers are obligated to provide sufficient detail in their reports to allow for reproducibility and independent verification of findings."}
policy.sources: {"name":"COPE Guidelines for Research Reporting","description":"Ethical guidelines from the Committee on Publication Ethics (COPE) for transparent and accurate reporting in research publications.","link":"https://publicationethics.org/"}; {"name":"OECD Principles on Research Accountability","description":"Recommendations for fostering accountability and transparency in research reporting.","link":"https://www.oecd.org/"}; {"name":"EOSC Reporting and Accountability Framework","description":"EOSC-specific guidance for reporting research activities and ensuring accountability in open science.","link":"https://eosc-portal.eu/"}

policy.description: Governance of Cross-Border Research Projects Rights define the permissions, prohibitions, and obligations for managing and governing research projects involving international collaboration. These rights emphasize compliance with legal frameworks, ethical standards, and effective coordination across jurisdictions.

policy.duties: {"action":"ensureLegalCompliance","description":"Researchers and institutions must comply with the laws and regulations of all involved jurisdictions to ensure the legality and integrity of cross-border research."}; {"action":"fosterTransparentCommunication","description":"Institutions must establish clear communication channels to coordinate activities effectively and transparently across borders."}
policy.sources: {"name":"OECD Guidelines on Cross-Border Collaboration","description":"Recommendations for managing international research collaborations effectively and ethically.","link":"https://www.oecd.org/"}; {"name":"General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR)","description":"European Union regulation addressing the transfer of personal data across borders.","link":"https://gdpr-info.eu/"}; {"name":"EOSC Framework for International Research Projects","description":"EOSC-specific guidance for governing cross-border research initiatives in open science.","link":"https://eosc-portal.eu/"}

policy.description: Standards for Open Data Reuse define the permissions, prohibitions, and obligations for ensuring that open data is reused ethically and effectively. These rights emphasize adherence to licensing terms, proper attribution, and compliance with technical standards for interoperability.
policy.permissions: {"action":"reuseOpenDataWithAttribution","constraint":{"name":"attributionRequirement","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"true"},"description":"Reusing open data is permitted, provided that proper attribution is given to the original source."}; {"action":"integrateOpenDataIntoResearch","constraint":{"name":"licenseCompliance","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"true"},"description":"Using open data in research projects is allowed, provided that licensing terms are respected and ethical guidelines are followed."}
policy.prohibitions: {"action":"ignoreLicensingTerms","constraint":{"name":"licenseAdherence","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Reusing open data in ways that violate its licensing terms is strictly prohibited."}; {"action":"misattributeDataSources","constraint":{"name":"sourceAcknowledgment","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Using open data without acknowledging the original source is prohibited and violates ethical standards."}
policy.duties: {"action":"ensureDataIntegrity","description":"Researchers must ensure that reused data remains accurate and unaltered in ways that could mislead or misrepresent its original context."}; {"action":"documentDataSources","description":"Researchers are obligated to document the sources of reused data to ensure transparency and reproducibility in their research."}
policy.sources: {"name":"FAIR Principles for Open Data","description":"Guidelines for ensuring that data is Findable, Accessible, Interoperable, and Reusable.","link":"https://www.go-fair.org/"}; {"name":"Creative Commons Licensing Standards","description":"Framework for applying licenses to ensure ethical reuse of open data and resources.","link":"https://creativecommons.org/"}; {"name":"EOSC Open Data Reuse Framework","description":"EOSC-specific guidance for promoting the reuse of open data in compliance with licensing and ethical standards.","link":"https://eosc-portal.eu/"}

policy.description: Equitable Access to Research Resources Rights define the permissions, prohibitions, and obligations for ensuring that research resources, including data, tools, and infrastructure, are accessible to all eligible users. These rights emphasize inclusivity, fairness, and the removal of barriers to access for underrepresented groups.
policy.permissions: {"action":"provideOpenAccessResources","constraint":{"name":"openAccessCompliance","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"true"},"description":"Making research resources available to all under open access principles is permitted and encouraged."}; {"action":"prioritizeAccessibilityForUnderservedCommunities","constraint":{"name":"equityCompliance","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"true"},"description":"Implementing policies that prioritize access for underrepresented groups is allowed to promote equity."}
policy.prohibitions: {"action":"restrictAccessUnfairly","constraint":{"name":"accessEquity","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Imposing unnecessary restrictions or biases that limit access to research resources is strictly prohibited."}; {"action":"favorSpecificGroupsWithoutJustification","constraint":{"name":"justificationRequirement","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Providing preferential access to specific groups without clear and equitable justification is prohibited."}
policy.duties: {"action":"promoteInclusivity","description":"Researchers and institutions must actively promote inclusivity and equitable access to research resources to support diverse participation."}; {"action":"removeBarriers","description":"Institutions are obligated to identify and remove barriers to access, ensuring that resources are available to all eligible users."}
policy.sources: {"name":"UNESCO Recommendations on Equity in Research Access","description":"Guidelines for promoting fairness and inclusivity in access to research resources.","link":"https://www.unesco.org/"}; {"name":"OECD Principles for Inclusive Research Access","description":"Recommendations for ensuring equitable access to research tools and infrastructure.","link":"https://www.oecd.org/"}; {"name":"EOSC Access and Inclusivity Framework","description":"EOSC-specific guidance for ensuring equitable access to resources in open science.","link":"https://eosc-portal.eu/"}

policy.description: Research Infrastructure Sustainability Rights define the permissions, prohibitions, and obligations for ensuring the long-term usability, efficiency, and sustainability of research infrastructure, including digital tools, platforms, and physical resources. These rights emphasize proactive maintenance, sustainable funding, and resource efficiency.
policy.permissions: {"action":"upgradeAndMaintainInfrastructure","constraint":{"name":"maintenanceCompliance","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"true"},"description":"Upgrading and maintaining research infrastructure to ensure its long-term usability is permitted and encouraged."}; {"action":"adoptSustainablePractices","constraint":{"name":"sustainabilityPrinciples","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"true"},"description":"Implementing practices that reduce environmental impact and enhance resource efficiency is permitted to support sustainability."}
policy.prohibitions: {"action":"neglectMaintenance","constraint":{"name":"infrastructureIntegrity","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Leaving research infrastructure unmaintained, leading to degradation or obsolescence, is prohibited."}; {"action":"useUnsustainablePractices","constraint":{"name":"sustainabilityCompliance","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Adopting practices that deplete resources or harm the environment without justification is strictly prohibited."}
policy.duties: {"action":"ensureStableFunding","description":"Institutions must secure stable funding sources to support the long-term sustainability of research infrastructure."}; {"action":"promoteResourceEfficiency","description":"Researchers and institutions are obligated to optimize the use of research infrastructure to reduce waste and maximize utility."}
policy.sources: {"name":"OECD Guidelines on Infrastructure Sustainability","description":"Recommendations for ensuring the long-term sustainability of research infrastructure.","link":"https://www.oecd.org/"}; {"name":"UNESCO Sustainability Framework for Research Infrastructure","description":"Global guidelines for maintaining sustainable research environments.","link":"https://www.unesco.org/"}; {"name":"EOSC Infrastructure Sustainability Framework","description":"EOSC-specific guidance for sustaining digital and physical research infrastructures in open science.","link":"https://eosc-portal.eu/"}

policy.description: Ethical Use of Research Metrics Rights define the permissions, prohibitions, and obligations for using research metrics responsibly and transparently. These rights emphasize fairness, accountability, and adherence to best practices to avoid misuse or misrepresentation of research performance.
policy.permissions: {"action":"useMetricsTransparently","constraint":{"name":"transparencyRequirement","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"true"},"description":"Using research metrics openly with clear definitions and context is permitted to ensure accountability and understanding."}; {"action":"benchmarkAgainstStandards","constraint":{"name":"standardCompliance","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"true"},"description":"Comparing research performance against recognized and standardized benchmarks is allowed to provide meaningful insights."}
policy.prohibitions: {"action":"manipulateMetrics","constraint":{"name":"integrityRequirement","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Manipulating or misrepresenting research metrics for personal or institutional gain is strictly prohibited."}; {"action":"relySolelyOnQuantitativeMetrics","constraint":{"name":"qualitativeInclusion","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Evaluating research quality based solely on numerical indicators without considering qualitative factors is prohibited."}
policy.duties: {"action":"ensureMetricAccuracy","description":"Researchers and institutions must ensure that metrics are accurate, transparent, and contextualized to provide reliable evaluations."}; {"action":"promoteResponsibleUse","description":"Institutions must establish and promote guidelines for the responsible and fair use of research metrics in evaluations."}
policy.sources: {"name":"Leiden Manifesto for Research Metrics","description":"Principles for the responsible use of research metrics in the evaluation of research performance.","link":"https://www.leidenmanifesto.org/"}; {"name":"DORA (San Francisco Declaration on Research Assessment)","description":"Guidelines promoting the use of qualitative assessment methods over inappropriate reliance on journal-based metrics.","link":"https://sfdora.org/"}; {"name":"OECD Best Practices for Research Evaluation","description":"Recommendations for ethical and effective evaluation of research using metrics.","link":"https://www.oecd.org/"}

policy.description: Public Engagement and Knowledge Dissemination Rights define the permissions, prohibitions, and obligations for effectively engaging the public and disseminating research knowledge. These rights emphasize transparency, accessibility, and inclusivity to ensure research benefits society.
policy.permissions: {"action":"shareResearchFindingsWithPublic","constraint":{"name":"accessibilityRequirement","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"true"},"description":"Communicating research outcomes to the public through accessible formats and channels is permitted to promote understanding and transparency."}; {"action":"organizePublicOutreachEvents","constraint":{"name":"engagementPurpose","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"true"},"description":"Conducting events that promote understanding and application of research knowledge is permitted and encouraged."}
policy.prohibitions: {"action":"concealResearchImpacts","constraint":{"name":"transparencyRequirement","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Withholding information about significant societal impacts of research is strictly prohibited."}; {"action":"useResearchForMisleadingPublicMessaging","constraint":{"name":"accuracyCompliance","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Disseminating information that misrepresents research findings is prohibited to maintain trust and credibility."}
policy.duties: {"action":"ensureAccessibility","description":"Researchers must present findings in a manner that is accessible to diverse audiences, including non-experts, to maximize societal impact."}; {"action":"fosterDialogue","description":"Institutions must create opportunities for two-way communication between researchers and the public to enhance understanding and collaboration."}
policy.sources: {"name":"UNESCO Guidelines on Public Engagement in Science","description":"Global recommendations for fostering public involvement and understanding in scientific research.","link":"https://www.unesco.org/"}; {"name":"OECD Best Practices for Science Communication","description":"Recommendations for effective communication of research to the public and policymakers.","link":"https://www.oecd.org/"}; {"name":"EOSC Public Engagement Framework","description":"EOSC-specific guidance for integrating public engagement into research dissemination efforts.","link":"https://eosc-portal.eu/"}

policy.description: Training and Capacity Building in Open Science Rights define the permissions, prohibitions, and obligations for providing training and fostering capacity building in open science. These rights emphasize skill development, inclusivity, and the promotion of open science principles among researchers and stakeholders.
policy.permissions: {"action":"organizeTrainingPrograms","constraint":{"name":"trainingPurpose","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"true"},"description":"Developing and conducting training programs on open science practices is permitted to enhance skills and knowledge."}; {"action":"distributeOpenEducationalResources","constraint":{"name":"openAccessCompliance","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"true"},"description":"Sharing educational materials under open access licenses is permitted to promote accessibility and learning."}
policy.prohibitions: {"action":"excludeUnderrepresentedGroups","constraint":{"name":"equityCompliance","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Excluding certain groups from training opportunities without valid reasons is prohibited to ensure inclusivity."}; {"action":"chargeUnjustifiedFeesForOpenResources","constraint":{"name":"openResourcePolicy","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Imposing fees on resources designated as openly accessible is prohibited to maintain fairness and accessibility."}
policy.duties: {"action":"ensureAccessibility","description":"Institutions must ensure that training materials are accessible to all participants, including those with disabilities."}; {"action":"promoteContinuousLearning","description":"Institutions must foster an environment that supports ongoing skill development and learning in open science practices."}
policy.sources: {"name":"UNESCO Recommendations on Open Science Education","description":"Guidelines for fostering education and capacity building in open science.","link":"https://www.unesco.org/"}; {"name":"OECD Framework for Research Training","description":"Recommendations for enhancing research training and capacity building globally.","link":"https://www.oecd.org/"}; {"name":"EOSC Skills and Training Framework","description":"EOSC-specific guidance for training and skill development in open science.","link":"https://eosc-portal.eu/"}

policy.description: Ethical and Inclusive Recruitment in Research Rights define the permissions, prohibitions, and obligations for ensuring fair, transparent, and inclusive recruitment practices in research. These rights emphasize diversity, compliance with legal and ethical standards, and the promotion of equal opportunities.
policy.permissions: {"action":"advertiseResearchOpportunitiesBroadly","constraint":{"name":"accessibilityRequirement","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"true"},"description":"Posting research opportunities in diverse and accessible channels is permitted to ensure broad outreach and inclusivity."}; {"action":"considerDiverseCandidates","constraint":{"name":"equityCompliance","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"true"},"description":"Actively considering candidates from underrepresented groups is permitted to promote diversity in research teams."}
policy.prohibitions: {"action":"discriminateInRecruitment","constraint":{"name":"nonDiscriminationRequirement","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Excluding candidates based on race, gender, age, disability, or other protected characteristics is strictly prohibited."}; {"action":"lackTransparencyInSelection","constraint":{"name":"transparencyRequirement","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Conducting recruitment processes without clear and transparent criteria is prohibited."}
policy.duties: {"action":"ensureEqualOpportunity","description":"Researchers and institutions must provide equal opportunities for all candidates, ensuring that no one is unfairly excluded."}; {"action":"documentRecruitmentProcesses","description":"Institutions must maintain records of recruitment activities to ensure accountability and compliance with ethical and legal standards."}
policy.sources: {"name":"UNESCO Guidelines on Diversity in Research Recruitment","description":"International recommendations for fostering diversity and inclusivity in recruitment practices.","link":"https://www.unesco.org/"}; {"name":"OECD Best Practices for Inclusive Hiring","description":"Recommendations for ethical recruitment practices that promote equity and fairness.","link":"https://www.oecd.org/"}; {"name":"EOSC Recruitment and Diversity Framework","description":"EOSC-specific guidance for ethical and inclusive recruitment in open science projects.","link":"https://eosc-portal.eu/"}

policy.description: Legal and Ethical Use of Research Funding Rights define the permissions, prohibitions, and obligations for ensuring that research funds are used responsibly, transparently, and in compliance with funding agreements. These rights promote trust, efficiency, and accountability in financial management.
policy.permissions: {"action":"allocateFundsAccordingToProposals","constraint":{"name":"proposalCompliance","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"true"},"description":"Using funds for activities and expenses explicitly stated in the approved research proposal is permitted."}; {"action":"adjustBudgetWithApproval","constraint":{"name":"authorizationRequirement","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"true"},"description":"Making budget adjustments with authorization from the funding body is permitted to accommodate project needs."}
policy.prohibitions: {"action":"misuseOfFunds","constraint":{"name":"grantCompliance","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Using funds for purposes not aligned with the grant agreement is strictly prohibited."}; {"action":"concealFinancialRecords","constraint":{"name":"transparencyRequirement","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Withholding or falsifying financial documentation related to funding is prohibited."}
policy.duties: {"action":"maintainAccurateFinancialRecords","description":"Researchers and institutions must keep detailed and accurate records of all financial transactions related to the research project."}; {"action":"provideRegularReports","description":"Researchers must submit financial and progress reports to funding bodies as required to ensure accountability and transparency."}
policy.sources: {"name":"OECD Principles for Financial Accountability in Research","description":"Recommendations for promoting ethical and efficient use of research funding.","link":"https://www.oecd.org/"}; {"name":"UNESCO Guidelines on Research Funding Ethics","description":"International guidance for ensuring ethical use of research grants and funds.","link":"https://www.unesco.org/"}; {"name":"EOSC Funding Accountability Framework","description":"EOSC-specific guidance for managing and reporting the use of research funding.","link":"https://eosc-portal.eu/"}

policy.description: Intellectual Property Rights in Collaborative Research define the permissions, prohibitions, and obligations for managing intellectual property in collaborative research projects. These rights emphasize fairness, transparency, and adherence to agreements to ensure equitable distribution of IP rights among participants.
policy.permissions: {"action":"sharePreExistingIP","constraint":{"name":"agreementCompliance","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"true"},"description":"Sharing pre-existing intellectual property under agreed terms for collaborative use is permitted and encouraged."}; {"action":"developJointIP","constraint":{"name":"mutualConsent","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"true"},"description":"Creating and managing jointly developed intellectual property with partners is allowed and requires mutual agreement."}
policy.prohibitions: {"action":"claimExclusiveOwnershipWithoutAgreement","constraint":{"name":"ownershipConsent","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Claiming sole ownership of jointly developed intellectual property without explicit consent from all contributors is prohibited."}; {"action":"useCollaborativeIPWithoutAuthorization","constraint":{"name":"usageConsent","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Using intellectual property developed in the collaboration without mutual agreement is strictly prohibited."}
policy.duties: {"action":"establishIPAgreements","description":"Collaborators must formalize intellectual property ownership, use, and sharing agreements before the project begins to ensure clarity and fairness."}; {"action":"respectContributorRights","description":"Researchers must acknowledge and respect the contributions of all collaborators to jointly developed intellectual property."}
policy.sources: {"name":"WIPO Guidelines on Collaborative IP Management","description":"International recommendations for managing intellectual property in collaborative projects.","link":"https://www.wipo.int/"}; {"name":"OECD Principles for Collaborative IP Sharing","description":"Guidance for equitable and transparent sharing of intellectual property rights in research collaborations.","link":"https://www.oecd.org/"}; {"name":"EOSC IP Management Framework","description":"EOSC-specific guidance for managing intellectual property in open science collaborations.","link":"https://eosc-portal.eu/"}

policy.description: Ethical Considerations in Research Partnerships define the permissions, prohibitions, and obligations for fostering ethical and equitable collaborations. These rights emphasize transparency, mutual respect, and adherence to ethical principles to ensure fairness among stakeholders.
policy.permissions: {"action":"establishAgreementsTransparently","constraint":{"name":"transparencyRequirement","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"true"},"description":"Creating partnership agreements with clearly defined roles and responsibilities is permitted to ensure transparency."}; {"action":"shareResourcesEquitably","constraint":{"name":"fairAllocation","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"true"},"description":"Allocating and sharing resources fairly among all partners is permitted to promote equity and collaboration."}
policy.prohibitions: {"action":"exploitPartnerContributions","constraint":{"name":"equityCompliance","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Exploiting the contributions of partners for personal or institutional gain is strictly prohibited."}; {"action":"excludeRelevantStakeholders","constraint":{"name":"inclusivityRequirement","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Omitting key stakeholders without justified reasons is prohibited to ensure fairness and inclusivity."}
policy.duties: {"action":"promoteEthicalPractices","description":"Partners must actively promote ethical practices and ensure compliance with legal and institutional standards throughout the collaboration."}; {"action":"fosterOpenCommunication","description":"Institutions and researchers must establish mechanisms for regular and transparent communication among all partners to maintain trust and alignment."}
policy.sources: {"name":"UNESCO Principles for Ethical Research Partnerships","description":"International guidance for ensuring fairness and ethics in research collaborations.","link":"https://www.unesco.org/"}; {"name":"OECD Guidelines for Research Collaboration","description":"Recommendations for fostering equitable and ethical partnerships in research.","link":"https://www.oecd.org/"}; {"name":"EOSC Partnership Ethics Framework","description":"EOSC-specific guidance for managing ethical considerations in research partnerships.","link":"https://eosc-portal.eu/"}

policy.description: Open Science Incentives and Recognition Rights define the permissions, prohibitions, and obligations for creating and managing systems that incentivize and acknowledge contributions to open science. These rights emphasize fair evaluation, transparency, and acknowledgment of collaborative and individual efforts.

policy.prohibitions: {"action":"overlookOpenScienceEfforts","constraint":{"name":"evaluationCompliance","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Disregarding contributions to open science in evaluations or assessments is strictly prohibited."}; {"action":"favorIndividualContributionsWithoutJustification","constraint":{"name":"equityRequirement","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Prioritizing individual efforts over collaborative contributions without valid reasons is prohibited to maintain fairness."}
policy.duties: {"action":"establishTransparentCriteria","description":"Institutions must create clear and transparent criteria for rewarding contributions to open science practices."}; {"action":"promoteEquitableRecognition","description":"Researchers and institutions must ensure equitable recognition of all contributors to open science initiatives, promoting collaboration and inclusion."}
policy.sources: {"name":"UNESCO Guidelines on Open Science Incentives","description":"International recommendations for fostering recognition systems for open science contributions.","link":"https://www.unesco.org/"}; {"name":"OECD Framework for Research Recognition","description":"Recommendations for creating incentives and recognition mechanisms in research.","link":"https://www.oecd.org/"}; {"name":"EOSC Rewards and Recognition Framework","description":"EOSC-specific guidance for incentivizing and acknowledging contributions to open science.","link":"https://eosc-portal.eu/"}

policy.description: Ethical and Legal Use of Artificial Intelligence in Research Rights define the permissions, prohibitions, and obligations for employing AI tools ethically and legally in research. These rights emphasize accountability, transparency, fairness, and adherence to applicable laws and ethical guidelines.

policy.prohibitions: {"action":"introduceBiasInAISystems","constraint":{"name":"biasPrevention","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Designing or deploying AI systems that introduce or perpetuate bias is strictly prohibited."}; {"action":"useAIForHarmfulPurposes","constraint":{"name":"ethicalUsage","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Using AI to produce results or applications that are deceptive, harmful, or unethical is prohibited."}
policy.duties: {"action":"ensureExplainabilityAndTransparency","description":"Researchers must ensure that AI systems are explainable and their decision-making processes are transparent to promote trust and accountability."}; {"action":"complyWithAIRegulations","description":"Researchers must adhere to legal and ethical standards, such as GDPR or the EU AI Act, in the development and deployment of AI systems."}
policy.sources: {"name":"OECD Guidelines on AI Principles","description":"Recommendations for the ethical and trustworthy use of AI in research and development.","link":"https://www.oecd.org/"}; {"name":"EU Artificial Intelligence Act (Draft)","description":"European Union regulation providing a legal framework for the ethical use of AI.","link":"https://digital-strategy.ec.europa.eu/"}; {"name":"EOSC Responsible AI Framework","description":"EOSC-specific guidance for the use of AI in open science research with an emphasis on ethics and compliance.","link":"https://eosc-portal.eu/"}

policy.description: Governance of Citizen Science Projects Rights define the permissions, prohibitions, and obligations for managing and supporting citizen science initiatives. These rights emphasize inclusivity, transparency, and the ethical involvement of non-professional scientists in research activities.
policy.permissions: {"action":"engageCitizenScientists","constraint":{"name":"informedParticipation","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"true"},"description":"Involving citizens in data collection, analysis, and other research activities is permitted to enhance participation and inclusivity."}; {"action":"shareResearchResultsWithParticipants","constraint":{"name":"transparencyRequirement","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"true"},"description":"Sharing findings and progress with citizen scientists is permitted to promote transparency and trust."}
policy.prohibitions: {"action":"exploitCitizenContributions","constraint":{"name":"acknowledgmentCompliance","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Exploiting the contributions of citizen scientists without proper acknowledgment is prohibited."}; {"action":"excludeRelevantCommunities","constraint":{"name":"equityCompliance","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Excluding communities or individuals without justified reasons is strictly prohibited."}
policy.duties: {"action":"provideTrainingAndResources","description":"Researchers must provide the necessary tools, training, and resources to ensure that citizen scientists can participate effectively."}; {"action":"respectPrivacyAndConsent","description":"Researchers are obligated to obtain informed consent and protect the privacy of citizen scientists involved in the project."}
policy.sources: {"name":"UNESCO Guidelines on Citizen Science","description":"International recommendations for fostering ethical and inclusive citizen science practices.","link":"https://www.unesco.org/"}; {"name":"OECD Framework for Public Engagement in Research","description":"Guidance for involving the public in scientific research and ensuring equitable participation.","link":"https://www.oecd.org/"}; {"name":"EOSC Citizen Science Framework","description":"EOSC-specific guidance for managing citizen science projects in open science environments.","link":"https://eosc-portal.eu/"}

policy.description: Data Ethics in Collaborative Research Rights define the permissions, prohibitions, and obligations for ensuring ethical practices in the collection, sharing, and use of data. These rights emphasize compliance with ethical standards, data privacy, and respect for contributors’ rights.
policy.permissions: {"action":"shareDataResponsibly","constraint":{"name":"ethicalAgreements","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"true"},"description":"Sharing research data among collaborators under ethical and legal agreements is permitted to promote trust and transparency."}; {"action":"useDataForAgreedPurposes","constraint":{"name":"purposeAgreement","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"true"},"description":"Using data only for purposes explicitly agreed upon by all collaborators is permitted to ensure compliance."}
policy.prohibitions: {"action":"misuseSharedData","constraint":{"name":"ethicalCompliance","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Using shared data in ways that violate ethical or legal agreements is strictly prohibited."}; {"action":"ignoreDataPrivacyStandards","constraint":{"name":"privacyCompliance","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Processing data without ensuring compliance with data privacy regulations is prohibited."}
policy.duties: {"action":"establishDataGovernancePolicies","description":"Researchers must develop and adhere to data governance frameworks that define ethical practices for data collection and sharing."}; {"action":"ensureTransparencyInDataUse","description":"Researchers are obligated to disclose how data will be collected, processed, shared, and stored to ensure ethical compliance."}
policy.sources: {"name":"FAIR Data Principles","description":"Guidelines for making data Findable, Accessible, Interoperable, and Reusable while respecting ethical standards.","link":"https://www.go-fair.org/"}; {"name":"General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR)","description":"European Union regulation outlining requirements for data privacy and protection.","link":"https://gdpr-info.eu/"}; {"name":"EOSC Data Ethics Framework","description":"EOSC-specific guidance for maintaining ethical standards in data collection and sharing.","link":"https://eosc-portal.eu/"}

policy.description: Transparency in Research Collaboration Agreements Rights define the permissions, prohibitions, and obligations for ensuring clarity, accountability, and mutual understanding in agreements governing research collaborations.
policy.permissions: {"action":"establishClearTermsAndConditions","constraint":{"name":"clarityRequirement","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"true"},"description":"Creating agreements with explicitly stated roles, responsibilities, and deliverables is permitted to promote understanding and trust."}; {"action":"makeAgreementsAccessibleToStakeholders","constraint":{"name":"stakeholderAccess","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"true"},"description":"Sharing the terms of collaboration with relevant stakeholders for review and oversight is permitted to ensure transparency."}
policy.prohibitions: {"action":"omitCriticalInformation","constraint":{"name":"informationCompleteness","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Excluding key details that impact the understanding of the agreement is strictly prohibited."}; {"action":"bypassStakeholderConsultation","constraint":{"name":"inclusivityRequirement","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Finalizing agreements without input from all relevant stakeholders is prohibited."}
policy.duties: {"action":"ensureMutualConsent","description":"Researchers must ensure that all parties understand and agree to the terms of the collaboration, fostering trust and accountability."}; {"action":"regularlyReviewAgreements","description":"Agreements must be reviewed periodically to ensure their relevance and compliance with changing project conditions and standards."}
policy.sources: {"name":"UNESCO Guidelines on Transparent Collaboration","description":"Recommendations for fostering transparency and accountability in research partnerships.","link":"https://www.unesco.org/"}; {"name":"OECD Framework for Research Agreements","description":"Guidance for creating clear and fair agreements in collaborative research.","link":"https://www.oecd.org/"}; {"name":"EOSC Collaboration Transparency Framework","description":"EOSC-specific guidance for ensuring transparency in research collaboration agreements.","link":"https://eosc-portal.eu/"}

policy.description: Ethical Considerations in Interdisciplinary Research Rights define the permissions, prohibitions, and obligations for addressing ethical challenges in collaborations that involve multiple fields of study. These rights emphasize inclusivity, mutual respect, and compliance with ethical standards across diverse disciplines.
policy.permissions: {"action":"integrateDiverseExpertise","constraint":{"name":"collaborationPurpose","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"true"},"description":"Involving experts from different disciplines to enhance research outcomes is permitted and encouraged."}; {"action":"shareKnowledgeAcrossFields","constraint":{"name":"knowledgeSharing","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"true"},"description":"Sharing knowledge and methodologies across fields to foster collaboration and innovation is permitted."}
policy.prohibitions: {"action":"disregardEthicalStandardsAcrossFields","constraint":{"name":"ethicalCompliance","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Overlooking ethical guidelines specific to participating disciplines is prohibited."}; {"action":"marginalizeContributionsFromOtherDisciplines","constraint":{"name":"inclusivityRequirement","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Devaluing or excluding the contributions of experts from different fields is prohibited."}
policy.duties: {"action":"fosterMutualUnderstanding","description":"Researchers must ensure clear communication and understanding among collaborators from different disciplines to promote effective collaboration."}; {"action":"ensureEthicalAlignment","description":"Researchers are obligated to align their practices with ethical standards applicable across all involved disciplines to maintain integrity."}
policy.sources: {"name":"UNESCO Guidelines on Interdisciplinary Research Ethics","description":"International recommendations for addressing ethical considerations in interdisciplinary research.","link":"https://www.unesco.org/"}; {"name":"OECD Principles for Collaborative Research Across Disciplines","description":"Guidance for fostering ethical and effective interdisciplinary collaborations.","link":"https://www.oecd.org/"}; {"name":"EOSC Interdisciplinary Ethics Framework","description":"EOSC-specific guidance for ensuring ethical practices in interdisciplinary research collaborations.","link":"https://eosc-portal.eu/"}

policy.description: Sustainability in Open Science Practices Rights define the permissions, prohibitions, and obligations for ensuring that research workflows and activities adhere to sustainable principles. These rights emphasize resource management, environmental considerations, and long-term planning.
policy.permissions: {"action":"adoptSustainableDataStorageSolutions","constraint":{"name":"environmentalCompliance","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"true"},"description":"Using data storage solutions that minimize environmental impact is permitted to promote sustainability in open science."}; {"action":"promoteEfficientResourceUse","constraint":{"name":"resourceOptimization","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"true"},"description":"Implementing workflows and practices that reduce waste and optimize resource utilization is permitted and encouraged."}
policy.prohibitions: {"action":"useEnvironmentallyHarmfulPractices","constraint":{"name":"sustainabilityCompliance","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Adopting practices that cause significant environmental damage without justification is prohibited."}; {"action":"neglectSustainabilityInResearchPlans","constraint":{"name":"planningRequirement","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Ignoring sustainability considerations in the planning and execution of research projects is strictly prohibited."}
policy.duties: {"action":"incorporateSustainabilityGoals","description":"Researchers must include sustainability objectives in their research design and execution to promote environmental responsibility."}; {"action":"reportOnEnvironmentalImpact","description":"Researchers are obligated to document and report the environmental impact of their research activities to foster accountability."}
policy.sources: {"name":"UNESCO Guidelines for Sustainability in Science","description":"International recommendations for integrating sustainability into scientific research.","link":"https://www.unesco.org/"}; {"name":"OECD Framework for Sustainable Research Practices","description":"Recommendations for promoting environmentally responsible and sustainable research workflows.","link":"https://www.oecd.org/"}; {"name":"EOSC Sustainability Framework","description":"EOSC-specific guidance for implementing sustainable open science practices.","link":"https://eosc-portal.eu/"}

policy.description: Accessibility and Inclusion in Open Science Rights define the permissions, prohibitions, and obligations for ensuring that open science practices accommodate diverse needs and foster equitable participation. These rights emphasize removing barriers and ensuring that all stakeholders can benefit from research activities.
policy.permissions: {"action":"provideAccessibleResearchOutputs","constraint":{"name":"accessibilityCompliance","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"true"},"description":"Ensuring that research outputs are available in accessible formats for individuals with disabilities is permitted to promote inclusivity."}; {"action":"promoteInclusiveParticipation","constraint":{"name":"equityCompliance","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"true"},"description":"Involving underrepresented groups in research activities and decision-making processes is permitted to foster inclusivity."}
policy.prohibitions: {"action":"excludeIndividualsWithoutJustification","constraint":{"name":"inclusivityRequirement","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Excluding individuals or groups from participation without valid reasons is strictly prohibited."}; {"action":"neglectAccessibilityRequirements","constraint":{"name":"accessibilityCompliance","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Publishing research outputs without ensuring compliance with accessibility standards is prohibited."}
policy.duties: {"action":"adoptUniversalDesignPrinciples","description":"Researchers must design research outputs and workflows to accommodate diverse needs and ensure accessibility for all."}; {"action":"ensureEquitableAccess","description":"Researchers and institutions must ensure that all individuals, regardless of background or ability, have access to research resources and participation opportunities."}
policy.sources: {"name":"UNESCO Guidelines for Inclusive Science Practices","description":"International recommendations for fostering accessibility and inclusion in scientific research.","link":"https://www.unesco.org/"}; {"name":"OECD Framework for Accessible Research","description":"Guidance for removing barriers to participation in research and ensuring equitable access.","link":"https://www.oecd.org/"}; {"name":"EOSC Accessibility and Inclusion Framework","description":"EOSC-specific guidance for promoting accessibility and inclusivity in open science.","link":"https://eosc-portal.eu/"}

policy.description: Intellectual Property and Open Data Licensing Rights define the permissions, prohibitions, and obligations for managing IP and applying licenses to open data. These rights emphasize transparency, compliance with licensing terms, and the promotion of open access while respecting creators’ rights.
policy.permissions: {"action":"applyOpenLicenses","constraint":{"name":"licenseCompliance","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"true"},"description":"Applying open licenses, such as Creative Commons, to research data and outputs is permitted to promote reuse and accessibility."}; {"action":"distributeDataUnderClearTerms","constraint":{"name":"transparencyRequirement","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"true"},"description":"Sharing data with clear terms of use to ensure compliance with licensing agreements is permitted and encouraged."}
policy.prohibitions: {"action":"violateLicensingTerms","constraint":{"name":"licenseCompliance","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Using or distributing licensed data in ways that breach the specified terms is strictly prohibited."}; {"action":"claimOwnershipOfLicensedData","constraint":{"name":"attributionRequirement","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Claiming ownership of data distributed under open licenses without proper attribution is prohibited."}
policy.duties: {"action":"ensureProperAttribution","description":"Researchers must provide proper attribution when using licensed data to respect the rights of creators."}; {"action":"promoteLicensingAwareness","description":"Institutions must ensure that all stakeholders are aware of and comply with applicable licensing terms to avoid misuse."}
policy.sources: {"name":"Creative Commons Licensing Standards","description":"Guidelines for applying open licenses to data and resources to promote ethical reuse.","link":"https://creativecommons.org/"}; {"name":"OECD Principles for IP Management in Research","description":"Recommendations for balancing intellectual property rights and open access.","link":"https://www.oecd.org/"}; {"name":"EOSC Licensing Framework","description":"EOSC-specific guidance for applying and managing open data licenses.","link":"https://eosc-portal.eu/"}

policy.description: Open Science Data Interoperability Standards define the permissions, prohibitions, and obligations for ensuring data compatibility and reuse across systems and disciplines. These rights emphasize the use of standardized formats, protocols, and practices to enhance data sharing and reuse.
policy.permissions: {"action":"useStandardDataFormats","constraint":{"name":"formatCompliance","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"true"},"description":"Storing and sharing data in widely recognized and interoperable formats is permitted to enhance data compatibility."}; {"action":"adoptCommonMetadataStandards","constraint":{"name":"metadataStandardsCompliance","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"true"},"description":"Using metadata standards such as Dublin Core or Schema.org is permitted to ensure data discoverability and usability."}
policy.prohibitions: {"action":"useProprietaryFormatsWithoutAccessibility","constraint":{"name":"accessibilityRequirement","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Storing data in proprietary formats that hinder sharing and reuse is strictly prohibited."}; {"action":"ignoreInteroperabilityBestPractices","constraint":{"name":"interoperabilityCompliance","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Neglecting recommended standards and practices for ensuring data compatibility is prohibited."}
policy.duties: {"action":"ensureMachineReadableData","description":"Researchers must ensure that data is machine-readable and compatible with interoperable systems to facilitate sharing and analysis."}; {"action":"promoteDataHarmonization","description":"Institutions must facilitate harmonization of data standards to support cross-disciplinary research and collaboration."}
policy.sources: {"name":"FAIR Data Principles","description":"Guidelines for ensuring data is Findable, Accessible, Interoperable, and Reusable.","link":"https://www.go-fair.org/"}; {"name":"W3C Data on the Web Best Practices","description":"Recommendations for publishing data on the web in ways that maximize interoperability and usability.","link":"https://www.w3.org/TR/dwbp/"}; {"name":"EOSC Interoperability Framework","description":"EOSC-specific guidance for implementing interoperability standards in open science.","link":"https://eosc-portal.eu/"}

policy.description: Research Data Lifecycle Management Rights define the permissions, prohibitions, and obligations for managing research data throughout its lifecycle. These rights emphasize data integrity, accessibility, and compliance with ethical and legal standards.
policy.permissions: {"action":"createAndCollectDataTransparently","constraint":{"name":"ethicalCompliance","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"true"},"description":"Creating and collecting data transparently, following ethical guidelines and ensuring informed consent, is permitted."}; {"action":"storeAndArchiveDataSecurely","constraint":{"name":"securityCompliance","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"true"},"description":"Storing and archiving data securely, ensuring long-term access and integrity, is permitted and encouraged."}
policy.prohibitions: {"action":"destroyDataWithoutJustification","constraint":{"name":"dataRetentionPolicy","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Destroying research data without a valid and ethical reason is prohibited."}; {"action":"mismanageDataSecurity","constraint":{"name":"securityBestPractices","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Mishandling or compromising the security of research data is prohibited."}
policy.duties: {"action":"ensureDataAccessibility","description":"Researchers must ensure that data is accessible throughout its lifecycle, following open access principles and legal requirements."}; {"action":"maintainDataProvenance","description":"Researchers must document and maintain the provenance of data, ensuring traceability and transparency."}
policy.sources: {"name":"FAIR Data Principles","description":"Guidelines for making data Findable, Accessible, Interoperable, and Reusable throughout its lifecycle.","link":"https://www.go-fair.org/"}; {"name":"OECD Guidelines for Data Management in Research","description":"Best practices for managing research data across its lifecycle.","link":"https://www.oecd.org/"}; {"name":"EOSC Data Lifecycle Management Framework","description":"EOSC-specific guidance for managing research data from creation to preservation.","link":"https://eosc-portal.eu/"}

policy.description: Data Quality Assurance and Control in Research Rights define the permissions, prohibitions, and obligations for ensuring the accuracy, consistency, and reliability of research data. These rights emphasize data quality validation, transparency, and adherence to industry standards.
policy.permissions: {"action":"performRegularDataValidation","constraint":{"name":"accuracyCompliance","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"true"},"description":"Conducting regular data validation checks to ensure the accuracy and consistency of data is permitted."}; {"action":"useProvenDataQualityFrameworks","constraint":{"name":"frameworkAdherence","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"true"},"description":"Adopting industry-standard data quality frameworks to ensure reliable data collection, analysis, and reporting is permitted."}
policy.prohibitions: {"action":"ignoreDataAccuracyStandards","constraint":{"name":"dataQualityCompliance","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Disregarding standards and practices that ensure data accuracy and reliability is prohibited."}; {"action":"manipulateDataToMeetExpectations","constraint":{"name":"integrityRequirement","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Altering or manipulating data to match pre-defined hypotheses or expectations is strictly prohibited."}
policy.duties: {"action":"documentDataQualityChecks","description":"Researchers must document the processes and methodologies used to ensure data quality, ensuring transparency and traceability."}; {"action":"ensureTransparencyInDataHandling","description":"Researchers must ensure transparency in how data is collected, processed, and validated throughout the research process."}
