policy.sources: {"name":"ISO 8000 Data Quality Standards","description":"International standards for ensuring the quality of data throughout its lifecycle.","link":"https://www.iso.org/iso-8000-data-quality.html"}; {"name":"OECD Best Practices for Data Quality Assurance","description":"Recommendations for maintaining high standards of data quality in research projects.","link":"https://www.oecd.org/"}; {"name":"EOSC Data Quality Framework","description":"EOSC-specific guidance for ensuring data quality in open science environments.","link":"https://eosc-portal.eu/"}

policy.description: Ethical Use of Research Methods and Tools Rights define the permissions, prohibitions, and obligations for ensuring integrity, transparency, and compliance with ethical standards when using research methodologies, technologies, and tools.
policy.permissions: {"action":"useProvenResearchMethods","constraint":{"name":"methodologyCompliance","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"true"},"description":"Applying established and validated research methods to ensure accurate and reliable results is permitted."}; {"action":"adoptEthicalToolsAndTechnologies","constraint":{"name":"ethicalCompliance","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"true"},"description":"Using tools and technologies that align with ethical standards and do not cause harm is permitted."}
policy.prohibitions: {"action":"useDeceptiveMethods","constraint":{"name":"integrityRequirement","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Using research methods that are designed to deceive or manipulate outcomes is prohibited."}; {"action":"misuseToolsForHarmfulPurposes","constraint":{"name":"harmPrevention","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Using research tools or technologies in ways that could harm participants, society, or the environment is prohibited."}
policy.duties: {"action":"ensureInformedConsent","description":"Researchers must ensure that all participants understand and agree to the methods and tools being used in the research."}; {"action":"promoteResponsibleUseOfTools","description":"Institutions and researchers must ensure that tools and methods are used responsibly, in compliance with legal and ethical standards."}
policy.sources: {"name":"OECD Guidelines for Ethical Research Methods","description":"Recommendations for applying ethical research methods to ensure integrity and fairness in research.","link":"https://www.oecd.org/"}; {"name":"UNESCO Ethics of Science and Technology","description":"International guidelines for maintaining ethics in the use of scientific methods and tools.","link":"https://www.unesco.org/"}; {"name":"EOSC Ethical Research Methods Framework","description":"EOSC-specific guidance for the ethical use of methods and tools in open science research.","link":"https://eosc-portal.eu/"}

policy.description: Open Science Communication and Public Engagement Rights define the permissions, prohibitions, and obligations for effectively communicating research outcomes and engaging the public. These rights emphasize transparency, accessibility, and the promotion of science to wider audiences.
policy.permissions: {"action":"disseminateResearchResultsPublicly","constraint":{"name":"accessibilityRequirement","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"true"},"description":"Sharing research findings through open-access platforms and public media is permitted to promote scientific transparency."}; {"action":"organizePublicEngagementActivities","constraint":{"name":"engagementPurpose","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"true"},"description":"Organizing events such as workshops, webinars, and discussions to engage the public in science is permitted and encouraged."}
policy.prohibitions: {"action":"withholdResearchResultsFromPublicView","constraint":{"name":"openAccessCompliance","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Restricting access to research outcomes without justification, especially when open access is possible, is prohibited."}; {"action":"misrepresentResearchFindings","constraint":{"name":"integrityRequirement","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Communicating research results in a misleading or unscientific manner is strictly prohibited."}
policy.duties: {"action":"ensureClarityAndAccuracyInCommunication","description":"Researchers must ensure that their communication is clear, accurate, and understandable to the general public to avoid misinterpretation."}; {"action":"fosterInclusivityInEngagement","description":"Researchers must ensure that engagement activities are inclusive, representing diverse community interests and ensuring accessibility for all."}
policy.sources: {"name":"UNESCO Guidelines on Science Communication","description":"Recommendations for effective and inclusive science communication.","link":"https://www.unesco.org/"}; {"name":"OECD Guidelines for Public Engagement in Science","description":"Framework for involving the public in scientific discourse and enhancing scientific literacy.","link":"https://www.oecd.org/"}; {"name":"EOSC Public Engagement Framework","description":"EOSC-specific guidance for engaging the public in open science activities.","link":"https://eosc-portal.eu/"}

policy.description: Research Ethics Committees and Oversight Rights define the permissions, prohibitions, and obligations for ensuring that research practices comply with ethical guidelines and legal standards through proper oversight and review by research ethics committees (RECs).
policy.permissions: {"action":"reviewResearchProposals","constraint":{"name":"ethicalReview","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"true"},"description":"Reviewing and approving research proposals to ensure compliance with ethical standards is permitted and encouraged."}; {"action":"monitorOngoingResearchProjects","constraint":{"name":"complianceMonitoring","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"true"},"description":"Monitoring ongoing research to ensure compliance with ethical standards is permitted as part of REC oversight."}
policy.prohibitions: {"action":"conductResearchWithoutRECApproval","constraint":{"name":"approvalRequirement","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Conducting or continuing research without approval from the REC, when applicable, is strictly prohibited."}; {"action":"ignoreEthicalGuidelines","constraint":{"name":"ethicalCompliance","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Ignoring ethical guidelines or legal requirements during research is prohibited."}
policy.duties: {"action":"ensureInformedConsent","description":"Researchers must obtain and document informed consent from participants in accordance with ethical guidelines to protect participants' rights and welfare."}; {"action":"promoteEthicalAwareness","description":"Institutions must ensure that researchers are aware of and adhere to ethical guidelines, maintaining high standards of research integrity."}
policy.sources: {"name":"Declaration of Helsinki","description":"Ethical principles for medical research involving human subjects.","link":"https://www.wma.net/what-we-do/medical-ethics/declaration-of-helsinki/"}; {"name":"OECD Best Practices for Research Ethics Committees","description":"Guidelines for establishing and operating research ethics committees in various research domains.","link":"https://www.oecd.org/"}; {"name":"EOSC Ethical Oversight Framework","description":"EOSC-specific guidance for ethical oversight in open science research projects.","link":"https://eosc-portal.eu/"}

policy.description: Research Integrity and Responsible Conduct Rights define the permissions, prohibitions, and obligations for upholding the highest standards of honesty, transparency, and accountability in research. These rights emphasize responsible conduct throughout the research process.
policy.permissions: {"action":"conductResearchHonestlyAndTransparently","constraint":{"name":"integrityCompliance","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"true"},"description":"Carrying out research in an honest and transparent manner, ensuring all methods, results, and conclusions are accurately reported, is permitted."}; {"action":"collaborateResponsibly","constraint":{"name":"ethicalCollaboration","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"true"},"description":"Collaborating responsibly with others while ensuring that ethical standards and personal integrity are upheld is permitted."}
policy.prohibitions: {"action":"fabricateOrFalsifyData","constraint":{"name":"dataIntegrity","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Fabricating or falsifying research data or results is strictly prohibited."}; {"action":"plagiarize","constraint":{"name":"authorshipCompliance","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Using others' work or ideas without proper attribution or permission is prohibited."}
policy.duties: {"action":"promoteEthicalResearchPractices","description":"Researchers must actively promote and adhere to ethical research practices and encourage others to follow these standards."}; {"action":"reportMisconduct","description":"Researchers must report any violations of research integrity, such as misconduct, fraud, or unethical practices."}
policy.sources: {"name":"Singapore Statement on Research Integrity","description":"Global guidelines for promoting research integrity and responsible conduct in scientific research.","link":"https://www.singaporestatement.org/"}; {"name":"OECD Guidelines for Research Integrity","description":"Recommendations for ensuring integrity in research processes and preventing misconduct.","link":"https://www.oecd.org/"}; {"name":"EOSC Research Integrity Framework","description":"EOSC-specific guidance for maintaining integrity in open science practices and ensuring responsible conduct.","link":"https://eosc-portal.eu/"}

policy.description: Open Science in Public Policy Rights define the permissions, prohibitions, and obligations for integrating open science principles into public policy. These rights emphasize the role of governments and policymakers in supporting and promoting open access to research data, resources, and findings.
policy.permissions: {"action":"supportOpenAccessLegislation","constraint":{"name":"policyCompliance","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"true"},"description":"Creating and enforcing policies that encourage or mandate open access to research publications and data is permitted."}; {"action":"promoteOpenScienceFunding","constraint":{"name":"fundingSupport","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"true"},"description":"Allocating funding to initiatives and projects that promote open science is permitted and encouraged."}
policy.prohibitions: {"action":"restrictAccessToPubliclyFundedResearch","constraint":{"name":"publicAccessCompliance","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Limiting access to research data and publications funded by public money without justified reasons is prohibited."}; {"action":"imposeBarriersToDataSharing","constraint":{"name":"dataSharingCompliance","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Creating regulations that unnecessarily limit data sharing or impede research collaborations is prohibited."}
policy.duties: {"action":"ensureTransparentPolicyDevelopment","description":"Policymakers must ensure that open science policies are developed transparently and involve all relevant stakeholders to ensure fairness and inclusivity."}; {"action":"encourageInternationalCollaboration","description":"Governments must promote international collaboration on open science initiatives, ensuring that policies align with global best practices and foster cross-border scientific cooperation."}
policy.sources: {"name":"UNESCO Recommendations on Open Science Policies","description":"Guidelines for integrating open science principles into national and international policy frameworks.","link":"https://www.unesco.org/"}; {"name":"OECD Principles for Open Science in Policy","description":"Recommendations for embedding open science principles in national science and technology policy.","link":"https://www.oecd.org/"}; {"name":"EOSC Policy Framework for Open Science","description":"EOSC-specific guidelines for implementing open science practices in public policy.","link":"https://eosc-portal.eu/"}

policy.description: Legal and Ethical Challenges in Open Science Rights define the permissions, prohibitions, and obligations for ensuring that research practices comply with legal frameworks, intellectual property rights, and ethical standards while maintaining openness and accessibility.
policy.permissions: {"action":"shareDataAndResultsOpenly","constraint":{"name":"legalCompliance","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"true"},"description":"Sharing data, research outputs, and findings openly, while respecting intellectual property and privacy laws, is permitted."}; {"action":"collaborateAcrossBoundaries","constraint":{"name":"jurisdictionalCompliance","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"true"},"description":"Engaging in cross-border collaborations while ensuring compliance with legal frameworks in different jurisdictions is permitted."}
policy.prohibitions: {"action":"breachIntellectualPropertyRights","constraint":{"name":"intellectualPropertyCompliance","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Sharing or using data without respecting copyright, patent, or other intellectual property rights is prohibited."}; {"action":"disregardParticipantPrivacy","constraint":{"name":"privacyCompliance","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Compromising the privacy of research participants or violating confidentiality agreements is prohibited."}
policy.duties: {"action":"ensureLegalCompliance","description":"Researchers must ensure that their research complies with relevant laws and regulations regarding data sharing, intellectual property, and privacy."}; {"action":"addressEthicalConcerns","description":"Researchers must proactively address ethical concerns related to open science, including the implications of data sharing and public engagement."}
policy.sources: {"name":"European Union General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR)","description":"Regulation for the protection of personal data and privacy within the European Union.","link":"https://gdpr-info.eu/"}; {"name":"OECD Guidelines on Legal and Ethical Issues in Open Science","description":"Best practices for navigating the legal and ethical challenges of open science, particularly with regard to data sharing, privacy, and intellectual property.","link":"https://www.oecd.org/"}; {"name":"EOSC Legal and Ethical Framework for Open Science","description":"EOSC-specific guidance for addressing legal and ethical challenges in open science practices.","link":"https://eosc-portal.eu/"}

policy.description: Intellectual Property and Open Science Collaboration Rights define the permissions, prohibitions, and obligations for managing intellectual property (IP) in open science collaborations. These rights ensure a balance between sharing research outputs and protecting intellectual property.
policy.permissions: {"action":"shareIPWithCollaborationPartners","constraint":{"name":"agreementCompliance","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"true"},"description":"Sharing intellectual property with research collaborators under agreed terms is permitted to foster collaboration."}; {"action":"applyOpenLicensesToSharedData","constraint":{"name":"licenseCompliance","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"true"},"description":"Applying open licenses, such as Creative Commons, to data shared within the collaboration is permitted to promote open access."}
policy.prohibitions: {"action":"restrictAccessToCollaborationIPWithoutJustification","constraint":{"name":"accessRestriction","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Restricting access to intellectual property developed within the collaboration without mutual consent is prohibited."}; {"action":"claimSoleOwnershipWithoutAgreement","constraint":{"name":"ownershipAgreement","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Claiming sole ownership of jointly developed intellectual property without prior agreement is strictly prohibited."}
policy.duties: {"action":"establishClearIPAgreements","description":"Collaborators must create clear agreements that define intellectual property ownership and usage rights before starting the research."}; {"action":"respectContributionsFromAllParties","description":"Researchers must respect the contributions of all collaborators to intellectual property and ensure fair distribution of rights."}
policy.sources: {"name":"WIPO Guidelines for IP in Collaborative Research","description":"International guidelines for managing intellectual property in collaborative research.","link":"https://www.wipo.int/"}; {"name":"OECD Principles for IP and Open Science","description":"Recommendations for managing intellectual property in open science while balancing open access and protection of creators’ rights.","link":"https://www.oecd.org/"}; {"name":"EOSC Intellectual Property Framework","description":"EOSC-specific guidelines for managing intellectual property in open science collaborations.","link":"https://eosc-portal.eu/"}

policy.description: Ethics of Public Data Sharing Rights define the permissions, prohibitions, and obligations for the ethical sharing of public research data. These rights emphasize the importance of respecting privacy, consent, and the potential impact on communities when making data publicly available.
policy.permissions: {"action":"shareAnonymizedDataPublicly","constraint":{"name":"privacyCompliance","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"true"},"description":"Sharing anonymized data publicly is permitted, provided that participant privacy is protected."}; {"action":"makeDataAvailableForPublicUse","constraint":{"name":"accessibilityCompliance","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"true"},"description":"Making data available for public use through open-access platforms is permitted, as long as privacy and consent are respected."}
policy.prohibitions: {"action":"shareIdentifiableDataWithoutConsent","constraint":{"name":"consentCompliance","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Sharing personally identifiable data without informed consent is prohibited."}; {"action":"ignoreEthicalGuidelinesInDataSharing","constraint":{"name":"ethicalCompliance","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Disregarding ethical considerations, such as participant confidentiality, when sharing data is prohibited."}
policy.duties: {"action":"ensureInformedConsent","description":"Researchers must obtain informed consent from participants regarding the use of their data in public sharing, ensuring that participants are aware of how their data will be used."}; {"action":"respectDataSecurityStandards","description":"Researchers must ensure that public data sharing complies with data security standards to protect sensitive information and prevent misuse."}
policy.sources: {"name":"OECD Guidelines on Data Privacy and Public Sharing","description":"Recommendations for ensuring data privacy and protection in the context of public data sharing.","link":"https://www.oecd.org/"}; {"name":"UNESCO Guidelines on Ethical Data Sharing","description":"International recommendations for sharing data ethically, especially in relation to public access and participant rights.","link":"https://www.unesco.org/"}; {"name":"EOSC Framework for Ethical Data Sharing","description":"EOSC-specific guidance on ethical data sharing practices in open science.","link":"https://eosc-portal.eu/"}

policy.description: Open Science and Research Collaboration Agreements Rights define the permissions, prohibitions, and obligations for ensuring fairness, transparency, and respect for intellectual property in research collaborations. These rights emphasize clear agreements between collaborators to facilitate effective and equitable scientific work.
policy.permissions: {"action":"draftCollaborativeAgreements","constraint":{"name":"agreementClarity","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"true"},"description":"Creating collaboration agreements that clearly define the roles, responsibilities, and intellectual property rights of all parties is permitted to ensure fairness."}; {"action":"shareResultsWithCollaborators","constraint":{"name":"collaborativeSharing","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"true"},"description":"Sharing research outcomes, data, and findings with collaborators as per the terms of the collaboration agreement is permitted to promote transparency and mutual benefit."}
policy.prohibitions: {"action":"violateAgreementTerms","constraint":{"name":"agreementCompliance","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Violating the terms of the collaboration agreement, including intellectual property rights and confidentiality clauses, is prohibited."}; {"action":"excludeCollaboratorsUnfairly","constraint":{"name":"equityCompliance","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Excluding collaborators from research activities or the benefits of the project without mutual agreement is prohibited to ensure fairness."}
policy.duties: {"action":"ensureFairDistributionOfBenefits","description":"Researchers must ensure that the benefits and outcomes of the research are fairly distributed among all collaborators, recognizing their contributions."}; {"action":"establishClearIPOwnership","description":"Researchers must clearly define the ownership and rights to intellectual property created during the collaboration to avoid conflicts."}
policy.sources: {"name":"OECD Guidelines for Research Collaboration Agreements","description":"Recommendations for creating fair and transparent research collaboration agreements.","link":"https://www.oecd.org/"}; {"name":"UNESCO Open Science Collaboration Framework","description":"International guidelines for fostering effective and ethical collaboration in open science.","link":"https://www.unesco.org/"}; {"name":"EOSC Collaboration and IP Management Framework","description":"EOSC-specific guidance for managing research collaborations and intellectual property in open science projects.","link":"https://eosc-portal.eu/"}

policy.description: Open Science and Public Engagement in Policy Rights define the permissions, prohibitions, and obligations for integrating public engagement into open science policies. These rights emphasize the need for policies that actively involve the public in scientific research and promote science communication for societal benefit.
policy.permissions: {"action":"fosterPublicParticipationInResearch","constraint":{"name":"participationCompliance","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"true"},"description":"Involving the public in the research process, including data collection, analysis, and dissemination, is permitted to enhance societal involvement in science."}; {"action":"promoteOpenScienceInPolicyDevelopment","constraint":{"name":"policyAdvocacy","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"true"},"description":"Advocating for policies that promote open access to research and encourage public participation in science is permitted and encouraged."}
policy.prohibitions: {"action":"excludePublicVoicesFromPolicyDevelopment","constraint":{"name":"inclusivityRequirement","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Excluding public input or participation in the development of open science policies is prohibited."}; {"action":"limitPublicAccessToResearchOutcomes","constraint":{"name":"accessibilityRequirement","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Restricting public access to research outcomes, particularly if funded by public money, is prohibited."}
policy.duties: {"action":"ensureInclusivityInEngagementActivities","description":"Policymakers must ensure that public engagement activities are inclusive and accessible to all groups, including underrepresented communities."}; {"action":"promoteScienceLiteracyAndAwareness","description":"Governments must encourage initiatives to improve public understanding of science and its relevance to daily life."}
policy.sources: {"name":"OECD Guidelines for Public Engagement in Science Policy","description":"Guidelines for promoting public engagement in science and the development of policies that support open science.","link":"https://www.oecd.org/"}; {"name":"UNESCO Recommendations on Open Science and Public Engagement","description":"International recommendations for fostering public engagement in open science and ensuring that policies reflect public interest.","link":"https://www.unesco.org/"}; {"name":"EOSC Public Engagement and Policy Framework","description":"EOSC-specific guidance for promoting public engagement in open science policy development.","link":"https://eosc-portal.eu/"}

policy.description: Legal Frameworks for Open Science and Data Sharing Rights define the permissions, prohibitions, and obligations for ensuring that open science and data sharing practices comply with legal standards, including data protection, intellectual property, and international agreements.
policy.permissions: {"action":"shareDataUnderLegalFrameworks","constraint":{"name":"legalCompliance","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"true"},"description":"Sharing research data according to relevant legal standards, including data protection and intellectual property laws, is permitted."}; {"action":"createDataSharingAgreements","constraint":{"name":"agreementClarity","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"true"},"description":"Establishing data-sharing agreements that clearly define the terms of access, use, and protection of shared data is permitted."}
policy.prohibitions: {"action":"shareDataInViolationOfLaws","constraint":{"name":"legalCompliance","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Sharing data in violation of laws, such as data protection regulations or intellectual property rights, is prohibited."}; {"action":"useDataWithoutProperAgreements","constraint":{"name":"agreementCompliance","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Using or sharing data without clear legal agreements and understanding of data ownership and usage rights is prohibited."}
policy.duties: {"action":"complyWithDataProtectionLaws","description":"Researchers must ensure compliance with data protection laws, such as GDPR, when handling personal data."}; {"action":"ensureTransparencyInLegalAgreements","description":"Researchers must ensure that all legal agreements related to data sharing are transparent, clear, and accessible to all stakeholders."}
policy.sources: {"name":"General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR)","description":"European Union regulation that governs the protection of personal data.","link":"https://gdpr-info.eu/"}; {"name":"OECD Guidelines on Legal Frameworks for Open Science","description":"Guidelines for establishing legal frameworks that support open science and data sharing.","link":"https://www.oecd.org/"}; {"name":"EOSC Legal Framework for Data Sharing","description":"EOSC-specific guidelines for ensuring legal compliance in open science and data sharing practices.","link":"https://eosc-portal.eu/"}

policy.description: The Future of Open Science in Global Research Rights defines the permissions, prohibitions, and obligations for advancing open science globally. These rights emphasize international collaboration, emerging technologies, and the promotion of open access to research across the world.
policy.permissions: {"action":"collaborateOnGlobalOpenScienceInitiatives","constraint":{"name":"collaborationCompliance","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"true"},"description":"Participating in and supporting international projects and platforms that promote open science is permitted to advance global collaboration."}; {"action":"adoptEmergingTechnologiesInOpenScience","constraint":{"name":"technologyCompliance","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"true"},"description":"Integrating emerging technologies such as AI and blockchain to enhance openness, transparency, and accessibility of research is permitted and encouraged."}
policy.prohibitions: {"action":"restrictGlobalCollaborationInOpenScience","constraint":{"name":"globalCollaborationCompliance","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Preventing or restricting international collaboration that advances open science principles is prohibited."}; {"action":"hinderAccessToOpenScienceToolsAndData","constraint":{"name":"accessibilityCompliance","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Imposing barriers to accessing open science tools, datasets, or platforms, particularly for underserved regions, is prohibited."}
policy.duties: {"action":"supportPolicyDevelopmentForOpenScience","description":"Researchers, governments, and institutions must actively participate in shaping policies that support the growth of open science, ensuring its global reach."}; {"action":"ensureEquityInOpenScienceAccess","description":"Institutions must ensure that open science tools, data, and platforms are accessible to researchers and communities worldwide, particularly in low-resource regions."}
policy.sources: {"name":"UNESCO Open Science Recommendations","description":"Global recommendations to promote open science and the international sharing of knowledge.","link":"https://www.unesco.org/"}; {"name":"OECD Principles for the Future of Open Science","description":"Framework for advancing open science in the future, including global collaboration and the use of emerging technologies.","link":"https://www.oecd.org/"}; {"name":"EOSC Global Open Science Framework","description":"EOSC-specific framework for promoting global open science and ensuring equal access to research tools and platforms.","link":"https://eosc-portal.eu/"}

policy.description: Integration of Open Science in National and Regional Research Policies Rights define the permissions, prohibitions, and obligations for incorporating open science principles into national and regional research policies. These rights emphasize the importance of developing policies that promote openness, transparency, and collaboration in scientific research.
policy.permissions: {"action":"developNationalOpenScienceStrategies","constraint":{"name":"policyCompliance","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"true"},"description":"Creating and implementing national or regional strategies that promote open access to research and data is permitted to advance open science."}; {"action":"encourageOpenScienceCollaborationAcrossRegions","constraint":{"name":"collaborationCompliance","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"true"},"description":"Fostering collaboration between countries and regions to advance open science initiatives is permitted to promote global engagement."}
policy.prohibitions: {"action":"restrictOpenAccessToPublicResearch","constraint":{"name":"accessRestriction","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Imposing restrictions on open access to publicly funded research, unless justified by privacy, security, or ethical concerns, is prohibited."}; {"action":"excludeUnderrepresentedRegionsFromOpenScienceInitiatives","constraint":{"name":"equityCompliance","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Excluding regions or countries from open science initiatives, particularly those with limited access to research infrastructure, is prohibited."}
policy.duties: {"action":"promotePolicyAlignmentAcrossBorders","description":"Policymakers must ensure that national and regional open science policies align with international best practices to foster global collaboration."}; {"action":"ensureFairAccessToOpenScienceResources","description":"Policymakers must ensure that researchers from all regions, particularly low-resource regions, have access to open science resources and platforms."}
policy.sources: {"name":"OECD Guidelines on Open Science Policies","description":"Recommendations for developing open science policies that encourage access to data, collaboration, and transparency.","link":"https://www.oecd.org/"}; {"name":"UNESCO Open Science and Policy Development Framework","description":"Global framework for integrating open science into national and regional research policies.","link":"https://www.unesco.org/"}; {"name":"EOSC Regional Open Science Framework","description":"EOSC-specific guidance for developing regional open science policies and collaborations.","link":"https://eosc-portal.eu/"}

policy.description: Future of Open Science: Emerging Trends and Technologies Rights define the permissions, prohibitions, and obligations for incorporating emerging technologies into open science practices. These rights explore the impact of technologies like AI, blockchain, and data analytics on research accessibility, data sharing, and collaboration.
policy.permissions: {"action":"adoptArtificialIntelligenceInOpenScience","constraint":{"name":"technologyCompliance","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"true"},"description":"Integrating AI technologies to enhance data analysis, research efficiency, and knowledge discovery in open science is permitted."}; {"action":"useBlockchainForDataProvenanceAndTransparency","constraint":{"name":"technologyCompliance","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"true"},"description":"Using blockchain technology for ensuring the integrity, traceability, and transparency of research data and workflows is permitted."}
policy.prohibitions: {"action":"useEmergingTechnologiesUnethically","constraint":{"name":"ethicalCompliance","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Using emerging technologies in ways that compromise the ethics, privacy, or transparency of open science practices is prohibited."}; {"action":"restrictAccessToDataWithEmergingTechnologies","constraint":{"name":"accessibilityCompliance","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Using emerging technologies to create barriers to data access or limit the sharing of scientific knowledge is prohibited."}
policy.duties: {"action":"ensureEthicalUseOfTechnologies","description":"Researchers and institutions must ensure that emerging technologies are used ethically, respecting privacy, fairness, and transparency."}; {"action":"promoteInclusivityInTechnologyDevelopment","description":"Policymakers must ensure that emerging technologies in open science are developed and used in ways that are inclusive, equitable, and accessible to all researchers, including those in low-resource settings."}
policy.sources: {"name":"OECD Guidelines on Emerging Technologies in Open Science","description":"Guidelines for integrating emerging technologies into open science in a responsible and ethical manner.","link":"https://www.oecd.org/"}; {"name":"UNESCO Recommendation on the Ethics of AI and Open Science","description":"International recommendations for the ethical use of AI and other technologies in open science.","link":"https://www.unesco.org/"}; {"name":"EOSC Framework for Emerging Technologies in Open Science","description":"EOSC-specific guidance on the use of emerging technologies, including AI and blockchain, to advance open science.","link":"https://eosc-portal.eu/"}

policy.description: Open Science in Education and Training Rights define the permissions, prohibitions, and obligations for incorporating open science into education and training. These rights emphasize the role of open science in enhancing educational practices, increasing access to research resources, and fostering collaboration.
policy.permissions: {"action":"integrateOpenScienceIntoCurriculum","constraint":{"name":"educationCompliance","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"true"},"description":"Including open science principles and practices in educational programs, especially in research methods and data management courses, is permitted to foster a culture of openness."}; {"action":"promoteOpenAccessLearningResources","constraint":{"name":"accessibilityCompliance","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"true"},"description":"Creating and sharing open educational resources (OER) to support learning and teaching in open science is permitted to increase accessibility."}
policy.prohibitions: {"action":"restrictAccessToEducationalMaterials","constraint":{"name":"openAccessCompliance","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Restricting access to open science-related educational materials, especially those that are publicly funded, is prohibited."}; {"action":"excludeOpenScienceTopicsFromEducation","constraint":{"name":"curriculumCompliance","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Excluding open science principles and topics from academic curricula, particularly in research-focused disciplines, is prohibited."}
policy.duties: {"action":"ensureInclusivityInEducationalResources","description":"Educational institutions must ensure that open science educational resources are accessible to a wide range of learners, including those from underserved regions or backgrounds."}; {"action":"supportSkillsDevelopmentForOpenSciencePractices","description":"Institutions and educators must provide training and resources to develop the skills necessary for engaging with open science, such as data management, collaboration, and publication practices."}
policy.sources: {"name":"OECD Guidelines for Open Science Education and Training","description":"Guidelines for incorporating open science principles into education and training programs to enhance knowledge sharing and research skills.","link":"https://www.oecd.org/"}; {"name":"UNESCO Recommendation on Open Science Education","description":"International recommendations for embedding open science into higher education and professional training.","link":"https://www.unesco.org/"}; {"name":"EOSC Education and Training Framework for Open Science","description":"EOSC-specific framework for training students and researchers in open science practices.","link":"https://eosc-portal.eu/"}

policy.description: Open Science for Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) Rights define the permissions, prohibitions, and obligations for aligning open science practices with the United Nations’ Sustainable Development Goals. These rights emphasize the potential of open science to contribute to global challenges through transparent and collaborative research.
policy.permissions: {"action":"contributeDataToGlobalChallenges","constraint":{"name":"sdgCompliance","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"true"},"description":"Contributing research data, tools, and findings to open platforms supporting the SDGs, such as climate action and public health initiatives, is permitted."}; {"action":"collaborateWithGlobalStakeholdersOnSDGs","constraint":{"name":"stakeholderEngagement","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"true"},"description":"Engaging with governments, NGOs, and international organizations on research projects that align with the SDGs is permitted to foster global collaboration."}
policy.prohibitions: {"action":"limitAccessToDataImpactingSDGs","constraint":{"name":"accessibilityCompliance","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Restricting access to research or data that is critical to addressing global challenges aligned with the SDGs is prohibited."}; {"action":"misuseSDGResearchForProfit","constraint":{"name":"ethicalResearch","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Misusing research outcomes or data that are meant to address SDGs for commercial or private profit without proper consent or attribution is prohibited."}
policy.duties: {"action":"alignResearchWithSDGPriorities","description":"Researchers must ensure that their research aligns with the relevant SDGs and contributes to global sustainability efforts."}; {"action":"ensurePublicEngagementInSDGResearch","description":"Institutions and researchers must ensure that SDG-related research involves and benefits all relevant stakeholders, including local communities and marginalized groups."}
policy.sources: {"name":"United Nations Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) Framework","description":"The UN framework outlining the 17 goals for addressing global challenges by 2030.","link":"https://sdgs.un.org/goals"}; {"name":"OECD Guidelines for Open Science and SDGs","description":"Recommendations for integrating open science practices into efforts to achieve the SDGs.","link":"https://www.oecd.org/"}; {"name":"EOSC SDG Framework for Open Science","description":"EOSC-specific framework for promoting open science in alignment with the SDGs.","link":"https://eosc-portal.eu/"}

policy.description: Open Science and Policy Advocacy Rights define the permissions, prohibitions, and obligations for advocating for open science policies at local, national, and international levels. These rights emphasize the importance of engaging policymakers, stakeholders, and the public in supporting open access, collaboration, and transparency in research.
policy.permissions: {"action":"advocateForOpenScienceLegislation","constraint":{"name":"policyAdvocacyCompliance","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"true"},"description":"Actively participating in the development and promotion of laws and regulations that support open science is permitted to foster a supportive environment."}; {"action":"organizePublicCampaignsForOpenScienceAwareness","constraint":{"name":"awarenessCampaignCompliance","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"true"},"description":"Creating and participating in campaigns that raise public awareness of the importance of open science and open access to research is permitted."}
policy.prohibitions: {"action":"restrictAdvocacyForOpenScience","constraint":{"name":"advocacyFreedom","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Restricting or silencing advocacy for open science principles or the dissemination of open science policies is prohibited."}; {"action":"misrepresentOpenSciencePractices","constraint":{"name":"misrepresentationCompliance","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Misrepresenting open science practices or policies to the public or policymakers is prohibited and can hinder progress."}
policy.duties: {"action":"engagePolicymakersInOpenScienceDialogue","description":"Researchers and institutions must actively engage with policymakers to ensure open science is prioritized in national and international research agendas."}; {"action":"ensureInclusivityInPolicyAdvocacy","description":"Advocacy efforts must ensure the inclusion of diverse voices, especially those from marginalized communities, in open science policy development."}
policy.sources: {"name":"OECD Principles for Open Science Advocacy","description":"Guidelines for promoting open science principles and practices in policy-making.","link":"https://www.oecd.org/"}; {"name":"UNESCO Open Science and Policy Advocacy Framework","description":"International framework for advocating for open science and ensuring global support for open science policies.","link":"https://www.unesco.org/"}; {"name":"EOSC Policy Advocacy for Open Science","description":"EOSC-specific guidelines for advocating for open science policies and legislation.","link":"https://eosc-portal.eu/"}

policy.description: Closing Remarks and Future Outlook for Open Science Rights define the permissions, prohibitions, and obligations for ensuring the continued growth and sustainability of open science. These rights emphasize the need for continued advocacy, global collaboration, and adaptation to future challenges.
policy.permissions: {"action":"promoteContinuousOpenSciencePractices","constraint":{"name":"advocacyCompliance","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"true"},"description":"Promoting and integrating open science principles in research and policy worldwide is permitted to maintain momentum in the open science movement."}; {"action":"fosterGlobalOpenScienceInitiatives","constraint":{"name":"globalCollaboration","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"true"},"description":"Supporting international collaborations that promote the continued development of open science practices globally is permitted to ensure worldwide impact."}
policy.prohibitions: {"action":"regressOpenSciencePractices","constraint":{"name":"progressCompliance","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Regressing or reversing the progress made in advancing open science, particularly in the face of new challenges, is prohibited."}; {"action":"limitFutureOpenScienceOpportunities","constraint":{"name":"innovationCompliance","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Limiting future opportunities for innovation and growth in open science by imposing unnecessary restrictions or barriers is prohibited."}
policy.duties: {"action":"ensureLongTermSustainabilityOfOpenScience","description":"Researchers, policymakers, and institutions must work together to ensure that open science practices are sustainable and scalable for future generations."}; {"action":"monitorAndAdaptOpenSciencePractices","description":"Continuously evaluate and adapt open science practices to keep up with technological advancements, societal changes, and global needs."}
policy.sources: {"name":"OECD Vision for the Future of Open Science","description":"OECD’s vision for the future of open science and how it will continue to shape research, innovation, and policy.","link":"https://www.oecd.org/"}; {"name":"UNESCO Global Outlook on Open Science","description":"UNESCO’s vision for the role of open science in global development and its future impact.","link":"https://www.unesco.org/"}; {"name":"EOSC Sustainability Framework for Open Science","description":"EOSC’s framework for ensuring the long-term sustainability and growth of open science.","link":"https://eosc-portal.eu/"}

policy.description: This policy enforces GDPR-compliant data usage by requiring that the only accepted legal basis is explicit consent (dpv:Consent). It ensures that users can access data only if they meet this legal constraint.
policy.permissions: {"action":"use","target":"https://example.org/data/study-consent","constraint":{"name":"dpv:hasLegalBasis","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"dpv:Consent"},"description":"Data can be used only when the legal basis is explicit consent, as defined by GDPR."}
policy.prohibitions: {"action":"use","constraint":{"name":"dpv:hasLegalBasis","operator":"neq","rightOperand":"dpv:Consent"},"description":"Data usage under any legal basis other than consent is prohibited."}
policy.duties: {"action":"ensureConsentProvenance","description":"The data controller must guarantee that consent has been validly obtained, recorded, and stored."}
policy.sources: {"name":"EDPB Guidelines on Consent","description":"Defines the criteria for valid GDPR consent.","link":"https://edpb.europa.eu/"}; {"name":"Data Privacy Vocabulary (DPV)","description":"Ontology for expressing privacy and legal constraints in data usage policies.","link":"https://harshp.com/dpv/"}

policy.description: This policy restricts data access to connectors that meet a specified security profile, such as TRUST_PLUS. Only trusted systems are permitted to use the dataset.
policy.permissions: {"action":"use","target":"https://example.org/data/study-secure","constraint":{"name":"eosc:hasSecurityProfile","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"TRUST_PLUS"},"description":"Access is permitted only to connectors with a security profile classified as TRUST_PLUS."}
policy.prohibitions: {"action":"use","constraint":{"name":"eosc:hasSecurityProfile","operator":"neq","rightOperand":"TRUST_PLUS"},"description":"Access is denied if the connector does not meet the TRUST_PLUS security level."}
policy.duties: {"action":"verifySecurityProfile","description":"The platform must verify that the connector’s declared security profile matches the required level before granting access."}
policy.sources: {"name":"IDSA Dataspace Connector – Usage Control","description":"Defines usage control elements including security profile verification in data spaces.","link":"https://international-data-spaces-association.github.io/DataspaceConnector/Documentation/v6/UsageControl"}; {"name":"ODRL Access Control Profile (OAC)","description":"An ODRL profile for expressing access control constraints like security profile requirements.","link":"https://besteves4.github.io/odrl-access-control-profile/oac.html"}

policy.description: This policy restricts dataset usage exclusively to research-related purposes, using purpose classification constraints to enforce it.
policy.permissions: {"action":"use","target":"https://example.org/data/study-research","constraint":{"name":"dpv:hasPurpose","operator":"odrl:eq","rightOperand":"dpv:AcademicResearch"},"description":"Use is permitted only if the declared purpose is academic research."}
policy.prohibitions: {"action":"use","constraint":{"name":"dpv:hasPurpose","operator":"odrl:neq","rightOperand":"dpv:AcademicResearch"},"description":"Use of the data for non-research purposes (e.g. marketing, commercial exploitation) is prohibited."}
policy.duties: {"action":"logPurposeDeclaration","description":"Requesters must declare their intended purpose and log it before gaining access to the dataset."}
policy.sources: {"name":"ODRL Access Control Profile (OAC)","description":"Defines how purpose-based access rules can be modeled using ODRL.","link":"https://besteves4.github.io/odrl-access-control-profile/oac.html"}; {"name":"Data Privacy Vocabulary (DPV)","description":"Ontology for classifying purposes like academic research or commercial use.","link":"https://harshp.com/dpv/"}

policy.description: This policy enforces role-based access control, allowing only individuals identified as researchers to use the dataset.
policy.permissions: {"action":"use","target":"https://example.org/data/study-rolebased","constraint":{"name":"dpv:hasRole","operator":"odrl:eq","rightOperand":"dpv:Researcher"},"description":"Access is permitted only to users with the role 'Researcher'."}
policy.prohibitions: {"action":"use","constraint":{"name":"dpv:hasRole","operator":"odrl:neq","rightOperand":"dpv:Researcher"},"description":"Users without the 'Researcher' role are not allowed to access the dataset."}
policy.duties: {"action":"verifyUserRole","description":"The platform must validate that the requester holds the role of Researcher before allowing access."}
policy.sources: {"name":"Data Privacy Vocabulary (DPV)","description":"Provides role-based classifications including 'Researcher'.","link":"https://harshp.com/dpv/#DPV-Role"}; {"name":"EOSC Interoperability Framework","description":"Defines role-based access requirements for EOSC-compatible services.","link":"https://www.eosc.eu/eosc-interoperability-framework"}

policy.description: This policy requires ethics committee or IRB approval prior to granting access to sensitive datasets involving human subjects or health-related data.
policy.permissions: {"action":"use","target":"https://example.org/data/study-ethics","constraint":{"name":"eosc:requiresEthicsApproval","operator":"odrl:eq","rightOperand":"true"},"description":"Access is granted only if documented ethics approval has been obtained."}
policy.prohibitions: {"action":"use","constraint":{"name":"eosc:requiresEthicsApproval","operator":"odrl:eq","rightOperand":"false"},"description":"Access is denied if the requester lacks ethics committee approval."}
policy.duties: {"action":"provideEthicsApprovalDocument","description":"The requester must submit a valid ethics approval certificate before accessing the dataset."}
policy.sources: {"name":"European Commission – Ethics in Social Science and Humanities","description":"Guidelines for ethical compliance and approval in Horizon Europe research.","link":"https://ec.europa.eu/info/funding-tenders/opportunities/docs/2021-2027/horizon/guidance/ethics-rules_horizon_en.pdf"}; {"name":"ELSI Guidelines for Research Data (BBMRI-ERIC)","description":"Ethical and legal guidance for data sharing in biomedical and health research.","link":"https://www.bbmri-eric.eu/services/elsi/"}

policy.description: This policy ensures that access to the dataset is permitted only if Technical and Organisational Measures (TOMs) are in place to safeguard data protection.
policy.permissions: {"action":"use","target":"https://example.org/data/study-security","constraint":{"name":"eosc:requiresTOM","operator":"odrl:eq","rightOperand":["encryption","accessControl","logging"]},"description":"Access is granted only if encryption, access control, and audit logging are in place."}
policy.prohibitions: {"action":"use","constraint":{"name":"eosc:requiresTOM","operator":"odrl:eq","rightOperand":"none"},"description":"Access is denied if no security measures are implemented."}
policy.duties: {"action":"documentTOMs","description":"The data processor must document and audit the technical measures applied before data is accessed."}
policy.sources: {"name":"European Data Protection Board – Guidelines on Data Security","description":"Outlines GDPR-compliant technical and organisational measures such as encryption and pseudonymization.","link":"https://edpb.europa.eu/our-work-tools/our-documents/guidelines/guidelines-0102020-measures-comply-data-protection_en"}; {"name":"ISO/IEC 27001:2022 – Annex A Controls","description":"Lists security controls required for compliance with ISO/IEC 27001.","link":"https://www.iso.org/standard/27001"}

policy.description: This policy enforces a maximum data volume restriction for each access request. It ensures controlled distribution and mitigates risks related to overexposure of sensitive datasets.
policy.permissions: {"action":"use","target":"https://example.org/data/study-volume","constraint":{"name":"eosc:dataVolumeMiB","operator":"odrl:lteq","rightOperand":100},"description":"Access is permitted if the requested volume is 100 MiB or less."}
policy.prohibitions: {"action":"use","constraint":{"name":"eosc:dataVolumeMiB","operator":"odrl:gt","rightOperand":100},"description":"Access is denied if the request exceeds 100 MiB of data."}
policy.duties: {"action":"monitorVolumeUsage","description":"The data platform must monitor the data volume per request and enforce the limit automatically."}
policy.sources: {"name":"ODRL Core Model","description":"Supports numeric constraints such as volume or size limitations in policy expressions.","link":"https://www.w3.org/TR/odrl-model/"}; {"name":"EOSC FAIR Metrics Framework","description":"Describes data access evaluation based on volume and FAIR principles.","link":"https://www.eosc.eu/sites/default/files/eosc-fair-metrics-framework.pdf"}

policy.description: This policy restricts data access to entities located within a specific geographic region, such as the European Union, to ensure jurisdictional and regulatory compliance.
policy.permissions: {"action":"use","target":"https://example.org/data/study-euonly","constraint":{"name":"odrl:spatial","operator":"odrl:eq","rightOperand":"eu"},"description":"Access is granted only if the requester is located within the European Union."}
policy.prohibitions: {"action":"use","constraint":{"name":"odrl:spatial","operator":"odrl:neq","rightOperand":"eu"},"description":"Access is denied to users outside of the European Union."}
policy.duties: {"action":"enforceLocationVerification","description":"The system must verify the physical or jurisdictional location of the requester before allowing access."}
policy.sources: {"name":"ODRL Core Vocabulary – spatial","description":"Defines spatial restrictions using the 'spatial' constraint in ODRL.","link":"https://www.w3.org/TR/odrl-vocab/#term-spatial"}; {"name":"European Commission – International Data Transfers","description":"Regulatory guidance for data access and transfers across jurisdictions under the GDPR.","link":"https://ec.europa.eu/info/law/law-topic/data-protection/international-dimension-data-protection_en"}

policy.description: This policy requires requesters to provide a verified digital identity issued by a recognized authority before accessing the dataset.
policy.permissions: {"action":"use","target":"https://example.org/data/study-identity","constraint":{"name":"dpv:hasIdentity","operator":"odrl:eq","rightOperand":"https://idp.example.org/identities/john-doe"},"description":"Access is allowed only if the requester's identity can be verified through a trusted provider."}
policy.prohibitions: {"action":"use","constraint":{"name":"dpv:hasIdentity","operator":"odrl:isNull","rightOperand":"true"},"description":"Access is denied if no identity is provided."}
policy.duties: {"action":"verifyDigitalIdentity","description":"The platform must verify the authenticity of the declared identity through an approved TSP (e.g., eIDAS-compliant IdP)."}
policy.sources: {"name":"Data Privacy Vocabulary (DPV)","description":"Provides the 'hasIdentity' property for associating agents with verified identities.","link":"https://harshp.com/dpv/#DPV-Identity"}; {"name":"eIDAS Regulation (EU 910/2014)","description":"Defines the framework for electronic identification and trust services in the EU.","link":"https://digital-strategy.ec.europa.eu/en/policies/eidas"}

policy.description: This policy ensures that access can be revoked by the data provider at any time, requiring the system to check revocation status before access.
policy.permissions: {"action":"use","constraint":{"name":"eosc:revocationStatus","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"active"},"description":"Access is allowed only if the resource has not been flagged as revoked."}
policy.prohibitions: {"action":"use","constraint":{"name":"eosc:revocationStatus","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"revoked"},"description":"Access is prohibited after a revocation has been issued."}
policy.duties: {"action":"enforceRevocationCheck","description":"The platform must check the revocation status of the data before granting access."}
policy.sources: {"name":"MyData Operator White Paper","description":"Describes trust and revocation mechanisms within personal data infrastructures.","link":"https://mydata.org/operators/"}; {"name":"ISO/IEC 29146:2016","description":"International standard for identity management including access revocation principles.","link":"https://www.iso.org/standard/45138.html"}

policy.description: Restricts data usage to a defined time interval. Access is permitted only within this period.
policy.permissions: {"action":"use","constraint":[{"name":"startTime","operator":"gteq","rightOperand":"2024-01-01T00:00:00Z"},{"name":"endTime","operator":"lteq","rightOperand":"2024-12-31T23:59:59Z"}],"description":"Access is allowed only within the defined time interval."}
policy.prohibitions: {"action":"use","constraint":[{"name":"startTime","operator":"gt","rightOperand":"2024-12-31T23:59:59Z"},{"name":"endTime","operator":"lt","rightOperand":"2024-01-01T00:00:00Z"}],"description":"Access is not allowed outside the validity window."}
policy.sources: {"name":"W3C ODRL Model – Constraint","description":"Defines temporal constraints in policy expressions.","link":"https://www.w3.org/TR/odrl-model/#constraint"}; {"name":"IDS Policy Model – Constraint","description":"Supports time-based policy enforcement in IDS environments.","link":"https://w3id.org/idsa/core/Constraint"}

policy.description: Each access to the dataset must trigger a notification to a pre-defined endpoint for logging or compliance purposes.
policy.permissions: {"action":"use","postDuty":[{"action":"notify","constraint":{"name":"notificationEndpoint","operator":"definesAs","rightOperand":"https://audit.example.org/notify"}}],"description":"Access is permitted only if a notification is sent to the endpoint."}
policy.prohibitions: {"action":"use","constraint":{"name":"notificationEndpoint","operator":"isNull","rightOperand":"true"},"description":"Access is denied if no notification endpoint is provided."}
policy.duties: {"action":"sendAccessNotification","description":"Notify the audit endpoint upon each access request to ensure accountability."}
policy.sources: {"name":"Fraunhofer IDS Usage Notification","description":"Defines usage notification behavior in IDS policies.","link":"https://www.fraunhofer.de/en.html"}; {"name":"W3C ODRL Model – Duty Pattern","description":"Specifies duties triggered by access, including notifications.","link":"https://www.w3.org/TR/odrl-model/#duty"}

policy.description: This policy limits the total number of times the dataset may be accessed, enforcing a strict upper threshold.
policy.permissions: {"action":"use","constraint":{"name":"odrl:count","operator":"lteq","rightOperand":5},"description":"Access is allowed if the total access count is less than or equal to 5."}
policy.prohibitions: {"action":"use","constraint":{"name":"odrl:count","operator":"gt","rightOperand":5},"description":"Access is prohibited if the total access count exceeds 5."}
policy.duties: {"action":"trackAccessCount","description":"The platform must monitor and record the number of accesses to enforce this constraint."}
policy.sources: {"name":"W3C ODRL Model – Count Constraint","description":"Defines how to express numeric usage limitations on actions.","link":"https://www.w3.org/TR/odrl-model/#constraint"}; {"name":"IDS Policy – N Times Usage","description":"Supports constraints on repeated access in IDS usage policies.","link":"https://w3id.org/idsa/core/Constraint"}

policy.description: Restricts dataset access to users within a specified geographic area, such as an EU member state.
policy.permissions: {"action":"use","constraint":{"name":"odrl:spatial","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"https://geoshapes.example.org/eu-member-states"},"description":"Access is allowed if the requester is within the defined spatial boundary."}
policy.prohibitions: {"action":"use","constraint":{"name":"odrl:spatial","operator":"neq","rightOperand":"https://geoshapes.example.org/eu-member-states"},"description":"Access is denied if the requester is located outside the approved area."}
policy.duties: {"action":"resolveUserLocation","description":"Verify the requester’s IP-derived or declared location before access is granted."}
policy.sources: {"name":"W3C ODRL Vocabulary – Spatial Constraint","description":"Defines how geographic location constraints can be applied to usage policies.","link":"https://www.w3.org/TR/odrl-vocab/#term-spatial"}; {"name":"GeoJSON Specification (RFC 7946)","description":"Standard format for encoding geographic data structures, often used for defining access geofences.","link":"https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc7946"}

policy.description: Permits access to the dataset only if the data will be deleted after usage within a defined time window.
policy.permissions: {"action":"use","postDuty":[{"action":"delete","constraint":{"name":"odrl:dateTime","operator":"lt","rightOperand":"2025-12-31T23:59:59Z"}}],"description":"Access is allowed only if the data is deleted before the specified date."}
policy.prohibitions: {"action":"retain","constraint":{"name":"odrl:dateTime","operator":"gteq","rightOperand":"2025-12-31T23:59:59Z"},"description":"Retaining data after the deadline is prohibited."}
policy.duties: {"action":"deleteAfterUse","description":"Ensure the dataset is removed after the allowed time window to comply with data lifecycle obligations."}
policy.sources: {"name":"Fraunhofer IDS – Usage Until Deletion","description":"Defines access policies with deletion duties in the IDS usage framework.","link":"https://www.fraunhofer.de/en.html"}; {"name":"W3C ODRL Vocabulary – Delete Action","description":"Specifies the deletion of data as a duty after use.","link":"https://www.w3.org/TR/odrl-vocab/#term-delete"}

policy.description: Only systems that meet the required security profile are permitted to access the dataset.
policy.permissions: {"action":"use","constraint":{"name":"ids:securityLevel","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"idsc:TRUST_SECURITY_PROFILE"},"description":"Access is granted if the system has the TRUST security profile or higher."}
policy.prohibitions: {"action":"use","constraint":{"name":"ids:securityLevel","operator":"lt","rightOperand":"idsc:TRUST_SECURITY_PROFILE"},"description":"Systems below the required security level cannot access the data."}
policy.duties: {"action":"validateSecurityProfile","description":"The platform must ensure that the requester's declared security profile matches the expected threshold."}
policy.sources: {"name":"IDS Security Profile – IDSA Core","description":"Defines security levels (BASE, TRUST, TRUST+) for systems in IDS architectures.","link":"https://w3id.org/idsa/core/"}; {"name":"W3C ODRL Vocabulary – Security Level","description":"Allows policies to include security-level-based constraints.","link":"https://www.w3.org/TR/odrl-vocab/#term-securityLevel"}

policy.description: Access to the dataset is allowed only if the system sends a usage notification to a specified endpoint.
policy.permissions: {"action":"use","postDuty":[{"action":"notify","constraint":{"name":"odrl:endpoint","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"https://example.org/usage-log"}}],"description":"Access is allowed when usage is reported to the designated endpoint."}
policy.prohibitions: {"action":"use","constraint":{"name":"odrl:endpoint","operator":"isNull","rightOperand":"true"},"description":"Access is denied if no notification endpoint is defined or triggered."}
policy.duties: {"action":"sendUsageNotification","description":"A notification about data access must be sent to a trusted logging or monitoring service."}
policy.sources: {"name":"Fraunhofer IDS – Usage Notification","description":"Explains notification-based duties for data usage enforcement.","link":"https://www.fraunhofer.de/en.html"}; {"name":"W3C ODRL Vocabulary – Notify Action","description":"Defines notification as a post-duty following a permitted action.","link":"https://www.w3.org/TR/odrl-vocab/#term-notify"}

policy.description: Restricts access to the dataset to specific pre-approved connectors identified by URI.
policy.permissions: {"action":"use","constraint":{"name":"odrl:system","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"https://approved.connector.org/id"},"description":"Access is granted if the request comes from a known, approved connector."}
policy.prohibitions: {"action":"use","constraint":{"name":"odrl:system","operator":"neq","rightOperand":"https://approved.connector.org/id"},"description":"Access is denied if the connector does not match the approved URI."}
policy.duties: {"action":"validateConnectorIdentity","description":"Ensure the identity of the connector matches the expected URI prior to granting access."}
policy.sources: {"name":"IDS Connector Restrictions – IDSA Core","description":"Defines access restrictions based on connector identity in the IDS architecture.","link":"https://w3id.org/idsa/core/"}; {"name":"W3C ODRL Vocabulary – System","description":"Allows policy constraints based on the identifier of the requesting system.","link":"https://www.w3.org/TR/odrl-vocab/#term-system"}

policy.description: Allows a dataset to be accessed a limited number of times. Once the maximum count is reached, access must be denied.
policy.permissions: {"action":"use","constraint":{"name":"odrl:count","operator":"lteq","rightOperand":"5"},"description":"Access is permitted up to 5 times."}
policy.prohibitions: {"action":"use","constraint":{"name":"odrl:count","operator":"gt","rightOperand":"5"},"description":"Access is denied if more than 5 requests have been made."}
policy.duties: {"action":"monitorAccessCount","description":"The system must keep track of how many times the dataset has been accessed."}
policy.sources: {"name":"IDS Policy – N Times Usage","description":"Defines constraints based on the number of times a dataset can be accessed.","link":"https://w3id.org/idsa/core/"}; {"name":"W3C ODRL Vocabulary – Count","description":"Specifies limits on the number of executions of an action.","link":"https://www.w3.org/TR/odrl-vocab/#term-count"}

policy.description: Grants access to the dataset only for a limited duration starting from the first use.
policy.permissions: {"action":"use","constraint":{"name":"odrl:elapsedTime","operator":"lteq","rightOperand":"PT10H"},"description":"Access is allowed for a maximum of 10 hours."}
policy.prohibitions: {"action":"use","constraint":{"name":"odrl:elapsedTime","operator":"gt","rightOperand":"PT10H"},"description":"Access is denied after 10 hours have passed."}
policy.duties: {"action":"trackElapsedTime","description":"Track when access starts to ensure duration is not exceeded."}
policy.sources: {"name":"IDS Pattern: Duration Usage","description":"Policy for enforcing a time-bound usage window.","link":"https://w3id.org/idsa/core/"}; {"name":"W3C ODRL Vocabulary – Elapsed Time","description":"Used to define temporal constraints on actions.","link":"https://www.w3.org/TR/odrl-vocab/#term-elapsedTime"}

policy.description: Access to the dataset is permitted only during a specific start and end date/time interval.
policy.permissions: {"action":"use","constraint":[{"name":"odrl:policyUsageTime","operator":"gteq","rightOperand":"2025-06-10T08:00:00Z"},{"name":"odrl:policyUsageTime","operator":"lteq","rightOperand":"2025-06-10T18:00:00Z"}],"description":"Access is permitted only between 08:00 and 18:00 UTC on 10 June 2025."}
policy.prohibitions: {"action":"use","constraint":[{"name":"odrl:policyUsageTime","operator":"lt","rightOperand":"2025-06-10T08:00:00Z"},{"name":"odrl:policyUsageTime","operator":"gt","rightOperand":"2025-06-10T18:00:00Z"}],"description":"Access outside the defined window is denied."}
policy.duties: {"action":"checkAccessWindow","description":"Validate the request timestamp against the allowed access interval."}
policy.sources: {"name":"IDS Pattern: Usage During Interval","description":"Defines usage permissions bounded by start and end timestamps.","link":"https://w3id.org/idsa/core/"}; {"name":"W3C ODRL Vocabulary – Policy Usage Time","description":"Temporal policy constraint based on defined start/end timestamps.","link":"https://www.w3.org/TR/odrl-vocab/#term-policyUsageTime"}

policy.description: This policy mandates that each access to the dataset must be logged to ensure accountability.
policy.permissions: {"action":"use","description":"Access is allowed if the system is configured to log the event after usage.","postDuty":{"action":"odrl:log","target":"https://clearinghouse.example.org/logs","description":"Logs the usage event to the specified endpoint."}}
policy.prohibitions: {"action":"use","constraint":{"name":"odrl:log","operator":"isNull","rightOperand":"true"},"description":"Access is not permitted if logging is not configured."}
policy.duties: {"action":"odrl:log","description":"Each access must be logged to the designated clearing house for traceability."}
policy.sources: {"name":"IDS Usage Pattern: Local Logging","description":"Mandates logging each access event to a clearing house.","link":"https://w3id.org/idsa/core/"}; {"name":"W3C ODRL Vocabulary – Log Duty","description":"Defines a logging obligation for usage actions.","link":"https://www.w3.org/TR/odrl-vocab/#term-log"}

policy.description: This policy enforces the duty to send a usage notification after each data access event.
policy.permissions: {"action":"use","description":"Access is allowed if a notification is sent to the defined endpoint after access.","postDuty":{"action":"odrl:notify","constraint":{"name":"odrl:target","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"https://notification.example.org/usage"},"description":"Sends access notification to the specified URL with access details."}}
policy.prohibitions: {"action":"use","constraint":{"name":"odrl:notify","operator":"isNull","rightOperand":"true"},"description":"Access is not permitted if no notification is configured."}
policy.duties: {"action":"odrl:notify","description":"Send a notification with usage metadata (user, resource, timestamp) to the specified endpoint."}
policy.sources: {"name":"IDS Usage Pattern: Remote Notifications","description":"Enables remote notification of data usage for monitoring and control.","link":"https://w3id.org/idsa/core/"}; {"name":"W3C ODRL Vocabulary – Notify Duty","description":"Specifies the requirement to notify upon usage.","link":"https://www.w3.org/TR/odrl-vocab/#term-notify"}

policy.description: This policy enforces a maximum number of allowed uses for a dataset, ensuring fair and controlled access.
policy.permissions: {"action":"use","constraint":{"name":"odrl:count","operator":"lteq","rightOperand":5},"description":"Access is permitted only if the requester has not exceeded 5 uses."}
policy.prohibitions: {"action":"use","constraint":{"name":"odrl:count","operator":"gt","rightOperand":5},"description":"Access is denied if the requester has already used the dataset 5 times."}
policy.duties: {"action":"trackUsageCount","description":"The platform must monitor and enforce the allowed number of uses per user or client."}
policy.sources: {"name":"IDS Policy Pattern: N Times Usage","description":"Restricts data usage based on a maximum usage count.","link":"https://w3id.org/idsa/core/"}; {"name":"W3C ODRL Vocabulary – Count Constraint","description":"Defines constraints based on the number of allowed actions.","link":"https://www.w3.org/TR/odrl-vocab/#term-count"}

policy.description: This policy permits access to data for a specific time duration (e.g., 24 hours) from the start of policy enforcement or data provisioning.
policy.permissions: {"action":"use","constraint":{"name":"odrl:elapsedTime","operator":"lteq","rightOperand":"PT24H"},"description":"Access is permitted for up to 24 hours from the time of provisioning."}
policy.prohibitions: {"action":"use","constraint":{"name":"odrl:elapsedTime","operator":"gt","rightOperand":"PT24H"},"description":"Access is denied once the 24-hour window has expired."}
policy.duties: {"action":"enforceAccessExpiry","description":"The system must automatically revoke access after the specified duration."}
policy.sources: {"name":"IDS Policy Pattern: Duration Usage","description":"Defines access limits based on duration from provisioning time.","link":"https://w3id.org/idsa/core/"}; {"name":"W3C ODRL Vocabulary – elapsedTime Constraint","description":"Used to define time-based access limits.","link":"https://www.w3.org/TR/odrl-vocab/#term-elapsedTime"}

policy.description: This policy defines a time window during which access to the resource is allowed. Access before or after this interval is forbidden.
policy.permissions: {"action":"use","constraint":[{"name":"odrl:policyUsageTime","operator":"gteq","rightOperand":"2025-01-01T00:00:00Z"},{"name":"odrl:policyUsageTime","operator":"lteq","rightOperand":"2025-06-30T23:59:59Z"}],"description":"Access is permitted between 1 January and 30 June 2025."}
policy.prohibitions: {"action":"use","constraint":{"name":"odrl:policyUsageTime","operator":"outside","rightOperand":["2025-01-01T00:00:00Z","2025-06-30T23:59:59Z"]},"description":"Access is denied if requested outside the specified interval."}
policy.duties: {"action":"enforceIntervalAccessControl","description":"The platform must evaluate and apply the defined access window."}
policy.sources: {"name":"IDS Policy Pattern: Usage During Interval","description":"Specifies valid time intervals for access to a resource.","link":"https://w3id.org/idsa/core/"}; {"name":"W3C ODRL Vocabulary – policyUsageTime","description":"Time interval during which the policy is active.","link":"https://www.w3.org/TR/odrl-vocab/#term-policyUsageTime"}

policy.description: This policy allows temporary data access within a time interval and requires the data to be deleted at a specified deadline.
policy.permissions: {"action":"use","constraint":[{"name":"odrl:policyUsageTime","operator":"gteq","rightOperand":"2025-01-01T00:00:00Z"},{"name":"odrl:policyUsageTime","operator":"lteq","rightOperand":"2025-01-31T23:59:59Z"}],"description":"Access is allowed during January 2025, before deletion occurs."}
policy.prohibitions: {"action":"use","constraint":{"name":"odrl:policyUsageTime","operator":"gt","rightOperand":"2025-01-31T23:59:59Z"},"description":"Access is denied after 31 January 2025."}
policy.duties: {"action":"odrl:delete","constraint":{"name":"odrl:executionTime","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"2025-02-01T00:00:00Z"},"description":"The data must be deleted on 1 February 2025."}
policy.sources: {"name":"IDS Policy Pattern: Usage Until Deletion","description":"Policy with access interval followed by deletion of the resource.","link":"https://w3id.org/idsa/core/"}; {"name":"W3C ODRL Vocabulary – postDuty with odrl:delete","description":"Defines duties that must be performed after permission execution, such as deletion.","link":"https://www.w3.org/TR/odrl-vocab/#term-postDuty"}

policy.description: This policy allows access only to specific connectors identified by URI.
policy.permissions: {"action":"use","constraint":{"name":"odrl:system","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"https://connector.example.org/id"},"description":"Access is permitted only if the request is issued by the authorized connector."}
policy.prohibitions: {"action":"use","constraint":{"name":"odrl:system","operator":"neq","rightOperand":"https://connector.example.org/id"},"description":"Requests from any other connectors are denied."}
policy.duties: {"action":"verifyConnectorIdentity","description":"The system must validate the identity of the requesting connector against the authorized list."}
policy.sources: {"name":"IDS Policy Pattern: Connector Restricted Usage","description":"Restricts access based on connector URI identity.","link":"https://w3id.org/idsa/core/"}; {"name":"W3C ODRL Vocabulary – odrl:system","description":"Specifies the system (e.g., connector) initiating the action.","link":"https://www.w3.org/TR/odrl-vocab/#term-system"}

policy.description: This policy permits data access only to connectors with a certified security profile.
policy.permissions: {"action":"use","constraint":{"name":"ids:securityLevel","operator":"eq","rightOperand":"https://w3id.org/idsa/code/TRUST_SECURITY_PROFILE"},"description":"Only connectors with the TRUST_SECURITY_PROFILE are permitted to access the resource."}
policy.prohibitions: {"action":"use","constraint":{"name":"ids:securityLevel","operator":"neq","rightOperand":"https://w3id.org/idsa/code/TRUST_SECURITY_PROFILE"},"description":"Connectors without the required security profile are denied access."}
policy.duties: {"action":"verifySecurityProfile","description":"The system must validate the connector's security profile claim before access is granted."}
policy.sources: {"name":"IDS Policy Pattern: Security Profile Restricted Usage","description":"Enforces access control based on certified security levels of connectors.","link":"https://w3id.org/idsa/core/"}; {"name":"IDS Code List: Security Profiles","description":"Defines the recognized security levels for connector certification.","link":"https://w3id.org/idsa/code/"}
